tables.description: This is the master table that holds various accessories required for transportation, pickup and delivery. An accessory differs from a special service in that the customer has to pay for using the accessories. An example of an accessory is a refrigerator unit in a truck, that is used to transport ice creams. The accessories defined can be linked at the item level or at the location level in GC3. The logic in GC3 checks to see if an accessory is required and thus, schedules and calculates the cost accordingly.

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID
fields.description: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID contains the unique identifier for the accessorial code used for the itinerary.

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_XID
fields.description: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_XID contains the unique external identifier for the accessorial code used for the itinerary.

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_DESC
fields.description: ACCESSORIAL_DESC contains a description of the accessorial on an itinerary.

fields.name: SEQUENCING_FACTOR
fields.description: This column would specify the sequence number for the given accessorial code and it would be used for sequencing the rating of the accessorial while rating.

fields.name: ROUNDING_TYPE
fields.description: The type of rounding that will occur during rating. Value are: N=no rounding,I=round to nearest interval,F=floor, and C=ceiling.

fields.name: ROUNDING_INTERVAL
fields.description: Numbers will be rounded (or truncated) to the nearest increment of this value.

fields.name: ROUNDING_FIELDS_LEVEL
fields.description: Determines which kinds of fields will be affected by the rounding settings during costing. Values are 0-7, where 0=all fields, 1=none, 2=numeric fields only, 3=unit fields (weight, etc.), 4=currency, 5=numeric and unit fields, 6=numeric and currency fields, and 7=unit and currency fields.

fields.name: ROUNDING_APPLICATION
fields.description: Determines when to apply the rounding rules. Values are A/N/C/O where A=always, N=never, C=conditionals only, and O=cost calculations only.

fields.name: DEFICIT_CALCULATIONS_TYPE
fields.description: When to apply deficit calculations. Values are Y/N/P where Y = Yes, N = No and P = Payant Pour

fields.name: PAYMENT_METHOD_CODE_GID
fields.description: payment method to apply to the shipment cost when the shipment action to apply payment terms is executed

fields.name: IS_FILED_AS_TARIFF
fields.description: Indicates if this accessorial cost has been filed as tariff. This is set to 'N' for new spot rates. Users may wish to set up a saved query to notify users if this rate is used.

fields.name: COST_TYPE
fields.description: Designates the type of the cost. C = Charge, D = Discount, P = Profile, E = External, L = Logic Parameter

fields.name: CALENDAR_ACTIVITY_GID
fields.description: Allows the user to select a calendar activity for the calendar validation test

fields.name: CHARGE_BREAK_COMPARATOR2_GID

fields.name: EXTERNAL_RATING_ENGINE_GID
fields.description: For cost_type of E, specifies the external rating engine gid

fields.name: EXT_RE_FIELDSET_GID
fields.description: For external rating engine gids of type GENERIC, this specifies the fieldset used to call out to the external engine

fields.name: COST_CODE_GID
fields.description: Tags a cost with an accessorial code. This does not indicate that the cost is an accessorial. It is a way of identifying groups of costs.

fields.name: LOGIC_PARAM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: For cost_type of L, specifies the logic parameter qualifier (of type Currency) which contains the cost value used as the charge multiplier.

fields.name: IS_ACTIVE
fields.description: When 'N', indicates accessorial cost is inactive and should not be used.

fields.name: RATE_UNIT_BREAK2_GID
fields.description: For rates using a 2-d profile, this will specify the second profile gid (the row).

tables.description: Table that stores accessorials to be defined globally. The global accessorial will only be applied if an accessorial with the same code exists at the rate_offering/rate_geo level which directs the rating process to use the global accessorial for it's cost. The global (default) cost will be used if the valid accessorial defined at the rate_offering or rate_geo has no entries for it's costs.

fields.name: LRT_INTERRUPTABILITY
fields.description: Interruptability support: NONE = task cannot be interrupted; INTERRUPTIBLE = task can be interrupted, likely ending in a failure state; RESUBMITTABLE: task can be resubmitted into it's origin queue for handling by another machine

fields.description: Type of action, 'C' Collective, 'R' Recursive, 'I' Independent or NULL

fields.name: IS_FINAL_STATE

tables.description: This table specifies the activity that can occur at a location. It is used in conjunction with the Calendar table to define the date time profile for the activity. Some examples of the activity include: CLOSED DISPATCH LIVELOAD LOAD OFFICEHOURS PICKUP RECEIVE etc.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_GID
fields.description: ACTIVITY_GID contains the type of activity for which a calendar was created. Types include: 'FOO2' ACTIVATE CLOSED DISPATCH FOO LIVELOAD LOAD OFFICEHOURS PICKUP RECEIVE

fields.name: ACTIVITY_XID
fields.description: ACTIVITY_XID is the unique identifier of a pre-configured field (that is not updateable by the user) of a list of activities in G-Log. Types include: 'FOO2' ACTIVATE CLOSED DISPATCH FOO LIVELOAD LOAD OFFICEHOURS PICKUP RECEIVE

fields.name: ACTIVITY_NAME
fields.description: ACTIVITY_NAME is a pre-configured field (that is not updateable by the user) that stores the list of activities in G-Log. The ACTIVITY_NAME can be: DRIVER DISPATCHING FOO ITINERARY ACTIVATED LIVE TRAILER LOADING LOADING FREIGHT OFFICE HOURS RECEIVING FREIGHT WAREHOUSE CLOSED DOOR WAREHOUSE PICKING

tables.description: This table is a link between the Activity and the Calendar entries in the GC3 system. An activity can have many calendar activities like many Pickup times. Similarly a calendar profile can have many activities, like activities during a working hour calendar profile can be Pickup, Dispatch etc. This there is a many to many relationship between calendar profiles and activities. This table resolves the many to many relationship.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_CALENDAR_ID
fields.description: ACTIVITY_CALENDAR_ID is the system-generated unique ID for ACTIVITY_CALENDAR. (IDs are internal system indexes; therefore, they are usually not recognizable to users.)

fields.name: CALENDAR_GID
fields.description: CALENDAR_GID is the unique identifier for a calendar. A calendar entry defines when certain activities can occur.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_GID
fields.description: ACTIVITY_GID is the unique identifier of an activity. This entry defines an activity with the time(s) when that activity can occur.

fields.name: IS_WORK_ON
fields.description: IS_WORK_ON is a flag describing whether a specific activity calendar is active. (Valid values are Y for yes (the calendar is active) or N for no (the calendar is not active).

fields.name: BEGIN
fields.description: BEGIN contains the time when an activity can start.

fields.name: DURATION
fields.description: DURATION contains the length of time an activity can take place.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_TIME_FACTOR
fields.description: Acitivity factor provides a multiplication factor for the activity.

tables.description: This table is used to store any exceptions to the normal activity and calendar profiles. For e.g., Thanksgiving and Christmas might require extra pickup and delivery activity times than the standard profiles.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_CALENDAR_OVERRIDE_ID
fields.description: ACTIVITY_CALENDAR_OVERRIDE_ID is a system-generated unique ID associated with an override of a calendar. For example, if a location is open on Wednesdays but during a particular week a holiday falls on Wednesday, a calendar override will define the location as "closed" for all activities on that day.

fields.name: CALENDAR_GID
fields.description: CALENDAR_GID is the unique identifier for a calendar. A calendar entry defines when certain activities can occur.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_GID
fields.description: ACTIVITY_GID is the unique identifier of an activity. This entry defines an activity with the time(s) when that activity can occur.

fields.name: START_DATETIME
fields.description: START_DATETIME contains the starting point for a calendar override; the date and time the override becomes effective.

fields.name: END_DATETIME
fields.description: END_DATETIME contains the endpoint for a calendar override; the date and time the override is no longer in effect.

fields.name: OVERRIDE_NAME
fields.description: OVERRIDE_NAME contains text to identify the calendar override. Examples include: Christmas, delivery, lunch, winter, etc.

fields.name: IS_WORK_ON
fields.description: IS_WORK_ON is a flag describing whether a specific activity override calendar is active. (Valid values are Y for yes (the override calendar is active) or N for no (the override calendar is not active).

tables.description: Content provided by GC3...maps the valid criteria that may be used to configure auto-assignment. Also provides the information to the auto-assign engine to get the data from the business object.

fields.name: AUTO_ASSIGN_CRITERIA_GID
fields.description: Id for the criteria that GC3 can look at to decide on auto assignments

fields.name: CRITERIA_TRANSLATION_GID
fields.description: Translation id for the auto assign criteria

fields.name: DATA_TYPE1
fields.description: Data type of the field to show when the user creates a list of items for the component; may be S/B/I/N/D/G/U/C/P/H/L/F for String, Boolean, Integer, Number, Double, Gid, UOM, Char, Percentage, Geo Hierarchy, Drop-down list, Fixed Values. If null, no field should be shown.

fields.name: GID_QUERY_CLASS1
fields.description: Query class to use if DATA_TYPE is a finder

fields.name: VALID_VALUE_TYPE1_GID
fields.description: If DATA_TYPE is F, indicates the key for the set of fixed values to be displayed in a picklist

fields.name: UOM_TYPE1
fields.description: Uom type to use if DATA_TYPE is a uom such as DISTANCE, CURRENCY, WEIGHT etc.

fields.name: DATA_TYPE2
fields.description: Same as DATA_TYPE1, but may be used if there is a second field for a single piece of criteria

fields.name: GID_QUERY_CLASS2
fields.description: Same as GID_QUERY_CLASS1, but may be used if there is a second field for a single piece of criteria

fields.name: VALID_VALUE_TYPE2_GID
fields.description: Indicates the key for the set of fixed values to be displayed in a picklist

fields.name: UOM_TYPE2
fields.description: Same as UOM_TYPE1, but may be used if there is a second field for a single piece of criteria

fields.name: DATA_FIELD1
fields.description: Field in the implied business object to get the data for the component

fields.name: DATA_FIELD2
fields.description: Same as DATA_FIELD1 but may be used if there is a second field for a single piece of criteria

fields.name: JAVA_CLASS
fields.description: Java class to get the data for the component if it is not directly accessible by a field name in the implied business object

fields.name: RESULT_HANDLER_CLASS
fields.description: Java class to handle checking the data from the business object against the list defined in the rule

tables.description: Header information for the auto-assign rule

tables.description: This table lets you specify that a given domain should be automatically granted access to a given table set in any new domains that are subsequently created.

This lets you specify that the given domain should automatically grant access to a given table set to all new domains that are subsequently created. The grant_to_subdomain_only column lets you indicate that the automatic grant should apply only to child domains of the grantor domain.

tables.description: This table is a copy of the BN_RANGE_NUMBER table to be used by the tool for testing the BNG. It allows testing to be completely decoupled from the live BNG data so as not to cause conflicts.

This is an internal table that is used to generate Business Numbers by the business generator application logic. The rules define how the business numbers are generated. Right now since only the picture string generator is implemented, only picture string definitions are used. The user need to supply the Business number type and the Context. The BN engine locates the best matching rule definition based on rule type, domain, and the current date. After the rule definition is located the context is used to generate the business number.

tables.description: This table is used to create the calendar profile entries for the user company. It specifies the schedules, time periods, holiday events etc. for the entire year for the company. These entries are used by the GC3 engine when scheduling the pickup or delivery of an order.

fields.description: CALENDAR_NAME is text that describes the purpose of the calendar. Examples include: office hours, summer calendar, pickup, etc.

fields.name: START_DATE
fields.description: START_DATE contains the effective date of the calendar.

fields.name: DAYS_IN_CYCLE
fields.description: DAYS_IN_CYLCE specifies the number of days for which this calendar is in effect.

fields.name: NOTES
fields.description: NOTES contains text that describes additional information about the calendar. Examples include: "ALL THE TIME," "CAL_ANNUAL," "OFFICE HOURS - 8AM TO 6PM," etc.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: This table defines whether two or more commodities are not compatible when transported together.

THIS TABLE STRUCTURE IS SIMILAR TO THE INVOICE_PROTECTIVE_SERVICE TABLE. PROTECTIVE SERVICE IS USED BY THE RAIL INDUSTRY TO COMMUNICATE TEMPERATURE REQUIREMENTS AND IS WHERE THE USER COMMUNICATES OPTIMAL TEMPERATURE FOR THE RR. THE USER CAN SPECIFY UP TO 5 CODES THAT ARE ALL USED TOGETHER, THIS IS WHY THERE IS A SEQUENCE NUMBER INCLUDED WITHIN THE STRUCTURE.

tables.description: This table is used to identify a contact person at a location for communication.

fields.description: CONTACT_GID contains the unique identifier for the contact for the location.

fields.name: CONTACT_XID
fields.description: CONTACT_XID contains the unique external identifier for the contact for the location.

fields.description: FIRST_NAME contains the given name of the contact person for the location.

fields.name: LAST_NAME
fields.description: LAST_NAME contains the surname of the contact person for the location.

fields.name: JOB_TITLE
fields.description: JOB_TITLE contains the title for the contact person at the location.

fields.description: EMAIL_ADDRESS contains the email address of the contact person for the location.

fields.name: PHONE1
fields.description: PHONE1 contains the primary telephone number for the location'scontact person.

fields.name: PHONE2
fields.description: PHONE2 contains the secondary telephone number for the location'scontact person.

fields.name: FAX
fields.description: FAX contains the facsimile number for the contact person for the location.

fields.description: Valid only if the contact_type = 'GROUP'. This is a boolean flag which controls whether GC3 creates a single notification message and broadcasts (i.e. cc) it to each group member or whether GC3 creates a message customized for each member.

fields.name: GROUP_SAVED_QUERY_GID

fields.name: LOCATION_GID

tables.description: Content Management System for document integration

fields.name: CONTENT_MANAGEMENT_SYSTEM_GID
fields.description: Content Management System GID

fields.name: CONTENT_MANAGEMENT_SYSTEM_XID
fields.description: Content Management System XID

fields.name: DESCRIPTION
fields.description: Description of system

fields.name: URL
fields.description: URL for system communication

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: Type of CMS: "OTM" = OTM is managing and storing documents within the schema. "WCC" - Oracle Webcenter Content is used to manage and store documents. "JCR" - Some other JSR-170 compliant system is used to manage and store documents.

fields.name: USER_NAME
fields.description: User name for remote authorization

fields.name: PASSWORD
fields.description: Credentials for remote authorization

fields.name: WCC_SUPPORTS_ACCOUNTS
fields.description: If "Y", WCC has been configured to support accounts.

fields.name: WCC_USE_DIRECT_URL
fields.description: If "Y", users receive direct URL's into WCC, necessitating an SSO solution or login to WCC. If "N", content is piped through OTM.

fields.name: WCC_USE_AUTO_GENERATED_ID
fields.description: If "Y", WCC has been configured to auto generated document ID's.

fields.name: WCC_SECURITY_GROUP
fields.description: The default security group for documents.

fields.name: WCC_DOCNAME_GEN_GID
fields.description: The default generator for the dDocName WCC parameter.

fields.name: WCC_DOCTITLE_GEN_GID
fields.description: The default generator for the dDocAccount WCC parameter.

fields.name: WCC_ACCOUNT_GEN_GID
fields.description: The default generator for the dDocAccount WCC parameter.

fields.name: WCC_REVLABEL_GEN_GID
fields.description: The default generator for the dRevLabel WCC parameter.

fields.name: WCC_REVIEW_MODE
fields.description: If "NONE", no document review workflows are used with the CMS. If "WCC", OTM forwards reviewers to WCC which is responsible for managing the review process. Users can accept or deny documents from OTM, but the action is forwarded to WCC. If "OTM", standard OTM review workflow is used. Documents cannot be accept or denied from WCC.

fields.name: WCC_DISTRIBUTION_MODE
fields.description: If "NONE", no document distribution is used with the CMS. If "WCC", OTM forwards distribution parties to WCC which is responsible for forwarding them finalized content. Users can finalize or unfinalize documents from OTM, but the action is forwarded to WCC. If "OTM", standard OTM finalization workflow is used. Documents cannot be finalized from WCC.

fields.name: JCR_CONNECTION_TYPE
fields.description: Type of connection to retrieve a JSR-170 Repository. "JNDI" assumes the content server supports JNDI lookup. "SERVICE LOADER" assumes libraries to support the content server are included with OTM. "JAVA" assumes a proprietary connection is needed.

fields.name: JCR_CONNECTION_PLUGIN_GID
fields.description: If the JCR connection type is "JAVA", the plugin to retrieve a JCR Repository object.

fields.name: JCR_SUPPORTS_REVISIONS
fields.description: If "Y", the JSR-170 compliant CMS supports document versioning

fields.name: ROOT_FOLDER
fields.description: Root folder for all OTM documents. If null, documents are assumed unfiled.

fields.name: FOLDER_TYPE
fields.description: Type of folders for document assignment. If NONE, documents are assumed unfiled. If FIXED, all documents are placed in the root folder. If DOMAIN, documents are placed in a subfolder of the root, equal to the domain. E.g., a document in domain A/B ,with a root of R, is placed in R/A/B. If GENERATED, the subfolder is determined by a parameter generator.

fields.name: FOLDER_GEN_GID
fields.description: The parameter generator for document subfolders.

fields.name: CMIS_BINDING
fields.description: CMIS Binding. If WEB SERVICES, Web Service calls over SOAP are used to invoke CMIS. If ATOM PUB, RESTful calls are used.

fields.name: CMIS_USE_VERSIONING
fields.description: If Y, the CMIS versioning implementation should be used when adding OTM revisions. Otherwise, an independent document is created for each revision.

fields.name: CMIS_NAME_GEN_GID
fields.description: Generator for the cmis:name parameter.

fields.name: CMIS_REVLABEL_GEN_GID
fields.description: Generator for the cmis:versionLabel parameter.

fields.name: CMIS_REPOSITORY_ID
fields.description: CMIS repository ID. If left blank, OTM will use the first repository found.

fields.name: FOLDER_CMS_TYPE
fields.description: Custom folder CMS type.

fields.name: DOCUMENT_CMS_TYPE
fields.description: Custom document CMS type.

fields.name: ODC_DOCTITLE_GEN_GID
fields.description: This column would store the param generator gid to generate title for documents

fields.name: JAVA_PLUGIN_GID
fields.description: Java implementation of interface to external system. Implements MessageSourceInterface.

fields.name: EXTERNAL_SYSTEM_GID
fields.description: Contains user and connectivity details for use by plugin to retrieve messages

fields.name: COM_METHOD_GID
fields.description: Communication method to use for transport

fields.name: SUBSCRIPTION_REFERENCE
fields.description: This is the reference provided when subscribing to a set of events in an external system.

fields.name: REQUEST_STYLESHEET_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: Stylesheet to apply to Request XML to generate vendor content request.

fields.name: RESPONSE_STYLESHT_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: Stylesheet to apply to the response of the request.

fields.name: SEND_ACK
fields.description: Specifies if an acknowledgement should be sent to the vendor confirming receipt of content.

tables.description: This table is used for the currency conversion in GC3. It stores the various countries and their currency names. The table Currrency_Exchange_Rate is used for the currency conversion logic.

fields.name: CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: CURRENCY_GID contains the unique identifier for the currency. Examples include USD for US dollars and DEM for Deutsche marks.

fields.name: CURRENCY_XID
fields.description: CURRENCY_XID contains the unique external identifier for the currency.

fields.name: CURRENCY_NAME
fields.description: CURRENCY_NAME contains the proper name for a currency. Examples include pesos and rupees.

fields.name: COUNTRY_CODE3_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

tables.description: This table is used for the currency conversion in GC3. It stores the conversion rates for every country with effective dates. It is used along with the Currency table to calculate the currency conversion rates.

fields.name: EXCHANGE_RATE_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: EFFECTIVE_DATE
fields.description: EFFECTIVE_DATE contains the date on which the currency exchange rate is applicable.

fields.name: FROM_CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: FROM_CURRENCY_GID contains the unique identifier for the currency to be calculated into a different currency.

fields.name: TO_CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: TO_CURRENCY_GID contains the unique identifier for the currency into which the original currency was calculated.

fields.name: TRIANGULATION_CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: EXCHANGE_RATE
fields.description: EXCHANGE_RATE contains the actual exchange rate.

tables.description: Defination of Cutoff Recovery Time details. The records can specify cutoff or recovery, and can indicate a day-time or an offset (not both).

fields.description: Hour adjustment from midnight.

fields.description: Minute Adjustment.

fields.name: IS_CUTOFF
fields.description: Indicates the record is defined for cutoff or recovery time. When 'Y', it's for cutoff time; when 'N', it's for recovery time.

fields.name: IS_OFFSET
fields.description: When 'Y', cutoff/recovery time is defined as offset; when 'N', cutoff/recovery time is defined as day-time.

fields.name: CUTOFF_TYPE
fields.description: Cutoff type. It can have one of the following values: CY: "Container Yard" - CY cutoff is the gate house time to arrive at the ocean carriers facility. SI "Shipping Instruction" - SI cutoff is the required time for shippers information to be provided to the forwarder. CFS: "Container Freight Station" - CFS Cutoff is the required time to be at a 3rd party location for container packing.

tables.description: This table is used as general references of glog object type. Agent and notification use DATA_QUERY_TYPE to identify glog object types.

fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: DATA_QUERY_TYPE_XID
fields.description: GC3 Unique identifier for the entity. Is used for creating the GID

fields.description: Allow to create a data query type without creating all of supported life time events

fields.description: If Y, the data query type supports text templates.

fields.description: If N, the business object should never be locked

tables.description: Defines a data queue

fields.name: DATA_QUEUE_DEF_GID
fields.description: Global ID for the data queue definition

fields.name: DATA_QUEUE_DEF_XID
fields.description: Data queue definition ID

fields.name: DATA_QUEUE_TABLE_GID
fields.description: Data table GID

fields.name: DATA_QUEUE_POLLER_GID
fields.description: Data queue poller GID

fields.name: DATA_QUEUE_EXECUTOR_GID
fields.description: Data queue executor GID

fields.name: THREAD_COUNT
fields.description: Number of threads to allocate to pollers

fields.name: BATCH_SIZE
fields.description: Number of events to retrieve in a polling batch. If null, all events are retrieved.

fields.name: POLLING_FREQUENCY
fields.description: Duration between queue polls

fields.name: FINDER_SET_GID
fields.description: Finder set for Event Queue viewer

fields.name: IS_ACTIVE
fields.description: If N, the queue is inactive and ignored by GC3

fields.name: LRT_RELATED_EVENT_QUEUE
fields.description: Event queue associated with the data queue. This allows the server to check event queue resources when pulling of records from the queue table.

fields.name: LRT_SCA_REQUIREMENTS
fields.description: For long running tasks, determines if the task should be pulled out of the data queue only when resources are available to process it. NONE=task is pulled regardless of resources; THREAD=task is pulled only if an event thread in the queue is available; MEDIATOR=task is pulled only if an event thread is available and the task will not be blocked by the mediator. Note this column is only used when LRT_RELATED_EVENT_QUEUE is not null.

fields.description: If Y, ACTIVE events should automatically be set to QUEUED on server restart

fields.name: REDO_FAILED_EVENTS
fields.description: If Y, FAILED events should automatically be set to QUEUED on server restart

tables.description: Domain is a concept in GC3 that creates a group per customer. When a customer (Shipper/ Service Provider) first installs GC3, it creates a domain for itself. All the subsequent data for that customer is then attached to the domain. This creates a logical grouping of the data per customer in GC3. This also allows GC3 to host multiple customers data simultaneously in one GC3 database. This concept can be thought of as a virtual data space per customer in GC3. The customers can provide access to their domain, to one or more service providers. This concept also allows to store similar codes between various customers, localized to their domain. The GID - Global Identifier is used in each and every table in GC3 to identify codes specific to GC3. The GID is formulated by concatenating Domain name and the XID as DomainName.XID An XID - External Identifier is used to store customer specific codes. For example Wal-Mart may give a code "Nike" to the Nike shoes in its inventory. K-Mart may also use the code "Nike" for Nike shoes in its inventory. For Wal-Mart: Domain Name: Wal-Mart Item_XID: Nike Item_GID: Walmart.Nike For K-Mart: Domain Name: Kmart Item_XID: Nike Item_GID: Kmart.Nike

tables.description: This table defines the categories that can be used for grouping the equipments. For example, the equipments can be grouped into: 42FT Covered Hopper, 60FT Flatcar, Frozen, Heated etc. The user can also record the size (length, height, width), weight and volume. A link to the defined temperature controls can also be established.

fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.description: GC3 Unique identifier for the entity. Is used for creating the GID

fields.name: EXPECTED_RESPONSE_TIME
fields.description: Used in notify as guaranteed delivery. Set the expected response time in second from receiving external system.

fields.name: IS_GUARANTEED_DELIVERY
fields.description: Flag that indicates whether the notification is a guaranteed delivery. A guaranteed delivery is a special type of notification where the receiver sends response back to the sender indicating whether the received message can be /or has been processed.

fields.name: SOCKET_TIMEOUT
fields.description: HTTP read/write timeount in seconds. A value of 0 means no timeout.

fields.name: ACTIVE_MODE
fields.description: Used to enable or disable sending to the external system. When set to Enabled it will generate and send the xml (default mode); Disable Send will generate and store the xml without sending; Disable Build will log an entry in the i_transmission and i_transaction tables without generating the xml blob; and Disabled will not log, generate, or send.

fields.name: IS_ENABLE_DEBUG
fields.description: Specifies if debugging is enabled for the external system.

fields.name: IS_LOG_RESPONSE_MSG
fields.description: Specifies if the response received should be logged.

fields.name: REATTEMPT_MODE
fields.description: Specifies the type of logic used to setup reattempts to send to the external system. If None, no reattempts are made. If Redo, then the status of the transmission will be set to REDO when an error occurs and the REDO transmission processing will manage a resend. If Delay, then the reattempt_delay field is used to indicate how long to wait before the next attempt.

fields.name: REATTEMPT_MAX
fields.description: Specifies the max number of attempts to send the transmission to the external system.

fields.name: REATTEMPT_DELAY
fields.description: Specifies the amount of time to wait before another attempt to send can be made.

fields.name: REATTEMPT_DEADLINE
fields.description: Specifies the max time from when the transmission is created that an attempt will be made to send the transmission.

fields.name: MAX_THREADS
fields.description: Specifies the max number of threads that should be used for this external system. This should help limit a bad destination taking up all the threads.

fields.name: FTP_APPEND_FILE
fields.description: Specifies if the FTP protocol should use the append file mode to send the data to the FTP server in batches, or should use the put mode where the whole transmission is sent at once.

fields.name: HTTP_ACK_EXPECTED
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission Ack is expected to be received when sending via synchronous protocol.

fields.name: HTTP_USE_ACK_STATUS
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission Ack status should be used as the status of the Transmission

fields.name: HTTP_READ_RESPONSE
fields.description: Specifies if the HTTP response conde should be validated.

fields.name: SERVICE_ACK_EXPECTED
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission Ack is expected to be received when sending via synchronous protocol.

fields.name: SERVICE_USE_ACK_STATUS
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission Ack status should be used as the status of the Transmission

fields.name: BPEL_ACK_EXPECTED
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission Ack is expected to be received when sending via synchronous protocol.

fields.name: BPEL_USE_ACK_STATUS
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission Ack status should be used as the status of the Transmission

fields.name: BPEL_DOMAIN_PW
fields.description: Specifies the password for the BPEL domain.

fields.name: BPEL_JNS_USER

tables.description: Main table that stores the list of KPIs.
This table in combination of its detail table, stores the USER_ENTERED target data. The table contains data for only those KPIs that have a TARGET_VALUE_TYPE of USER_ENTERED

fields.name: KPI_TARGET_VALUE_TYPE_ID
fields.description: With this column the KPI_TARGET_VALUE_STAGE table is folded under the E_KPI_TARGET_VALUE_TYPE table when the Target Value Type is 'User Entered'

tables.description: This table in combination of its header table, stores the USER_ENTERED target data. The table contains data for only those KPIs that have a TARGET_VALUE_TYPE of USER_ENTERED

This table defines the target value type for each KPIs target type. Example, KPI (Tender Performance) TARGET is FIXED 97%, or KPI (Tender Performance) FORECAST is FIXED 100%, or KPI (Cost Per Lane) TARGET is USER_DEFINED. For FIXED type, TARGET_VALUE should stores the fixed value. For MOVING_AVERAGE, TARGET_VALUE stores the number of months.
This table stores the various types of targets in the system. Example, TARGET, INDUSTRY_BENCHMARK, FORECAST. This table should usually only contain PUBLIC data.

This table stores the types of values that the Targets can contain. Example, the targets can be FIXED values (97% Tender performance always), MOVING_AVERAGE (Target for this month is the Average of last 3 months actuals), or USER_ENTERED (manually entered by the user for each period). This table should usually only contain PUBLIC data.

Holds configurable parameters for GC3 account policies. This includes when GC3 user'spasswords should expire, whether to maintain a login history for a user, etc. The policy is then associated to a user account within the GL_USER table.

fields.name: GL_ACCOUNT_POLICY_XID
fields.description: The external identifier for the account policy.

fields.name: PW_EXPIRATION_DURATION
fields.description: Identifies a length of time (in days) before a new password will expire. A null value indicates the password never expires.

fields.name: PW_EXPIRATION_WARNING_PERIOD
fields.description: Identifies the length of time(in days) prior to a user'spassword expiration in which the user should receive password expiration messages.

fields.name: NUM_PW_FOR_HISTORY
fields.description: The number of passwords which should be kept within the history for each user. The user will not be able to set his password to any password that is kept within the history.

fields.name: PW_CHANGE_AFTER_RESET
fields.description: Determines whether or not a password preset by the system or reset by an account ADMIN user via the User Manager screen will expire upon the first user login.

fields.name: MAX_ATTEMPTS_ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT
fields.description: The maximum number of login attempts allowed before an account is locked out. If the number is zero, then there is no lockout.

fields.name: LOCKOUT_DURATION
fields.description: How long to lock out an account after exceeding allowed login attempts.

fields.name: LOCKOUT_DURATION_UOM_CODE
fields.description: The unit of measure for the lockout duration.

fields.name: LOCKOUT_DURATION_BASE
fields.description: The lockout duration in base units.

fields.name: MAX_DAYS_ACCOUNT_DORMANCY
fields.description: Allowed inactive duration (in days) before an account is disabled.

fields.name: KEEP_LOGIN_HISTORY
fields.description: Whether or not to maintain a login history for the users.

tables.description: The goods_in_transit table indicates which packaged_items are currently in-transit between a given source and destination, and indicates the shipment(s) on which the items are moving. This table is maintained behind-the-scenes via a database trigger on the shipment_status table. Rows are added to the goods_in_transit table when a shipment'sgoods_in_transit status changes from from goods_in_transit_completed to goods_in_transit_in_transit. Rows are deleted from the goods_in_transit table when a shipment'sgoods_in_transit status changes from goods_in_transit_in_transit to goods_in_transit_completed. This table is queried by the visibility->inventory function.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

fields.name: DESCRIPTION
fields.description: Description of the Constraint Type. This contains details of how a Constraint of this type will be used for matching and is displayed on the UI against each Constraint detail .

fields.name: D_LIST_TYPE
fields.description: Indicates the detail type of the Constraint. Possible values : G,D,P,T,F. G- GID type. Used when Constraint requires no further for matching. Eg: For Constraint Item, Item ID will be considered. D- Droplist detail type for Qualifiers. Used when Constraint requires a list of Qualifiers for matching. Selection of Qualifiers can be done using Droplist. eg: For Constraint 'Refnum', Refnum Qualifiers can be entered using a droplist. P- Picklist detail type for Qualifiers. Used when Constraint requires a list of Qualifiers for matching. Selection of Qualifiers in this case can be done using Picklist T- Type detail type. Used to specify Product classification type for Line Classification Code Constraint. F- F is used for Fixed List.

fields.name: D_LIST_QUERY_CLASS
fields.description: Indicates the query class name of the Qualifier for entering Constraint Details List. This column is applicable only when the Constraint Type requires Qualifiers to be specified as Constraint Details (ie) Non Gid Type. Eg: REFNUM , REMARK, INVOLVED PARTY

fields.name: D_LIST_LABEL
fields.description: Indicates the label to be used on the Qualifier grid to enter Constraint Details. This column is applicable only when the Constraint Type requires Qualifiers to be specified as Constraint Details (ie) Non Gid Type. Eg: REFNUM , REMARK, INVOLVED PARTY

fields.name: D_LIST_VALID_VALUE_TYPE_GID
fields.description: This columns helps to maintain the valid values when d_list_type is F in the VALID_VALUE_TYPE table.

fields.name: D_LIST_TYPE2
fields.description: Indicates the detail type of the Constraint. Possible values : G,D,P,T,F. G- GID type. Used when Constraint requires no further for matching. D- Droplist detail type for Qualifiers. Used when Constraint requires a list of Qualifiers for matching. P- Picklist detail type for Qualifiers. Used when Constraint requires a list of Qualifiers for matching. T- Type detail type. Used to specify Product classification type for Line Classification Code Constraint. F- F is used for Fixed List.

fields.name: SOURCE_QUERY_CLASS2
fields.description: This filed indicates the query class name of the source qualifier. This is required in case the value of the field source_list_type is G or P

fields.name: TARGET_LIST_TYPE1
fields.description: This field indicates the type of the qualifier for target attribute value. The values can be G/D/P/F. G in case the attribute is a simple attribute. D is used for drop list. P is used for picklist.F is used for Fixed List.

fields.name: TARGET_QUERY_CLASS1
fields.description: This filed indicates the query class name of the target qualifier. This is required in case the value of the field target_list_type is G or P

fields.name: TARGET_LIST_TYPE2
fields.description: This field indicates the type of the qualifier for target attribute value. The values can be G/D/P/F. G in case the attribute is a simple attribute. D is used for drop list. P is used for picklist.F is used for Fixed List.

fields.name: TARGET_QUERY_CLASS2
fields.description: This filed indicates the query class name of the target qualifier. This is required in case the value of the field target_list_type is G or P

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

fields.name: IS_ALL_PACKED
fields.description: Flag to indicate the pkg is 'All packed in one'

fields.name: AUTHORIZATION
fields.description: The authorization for the Dangerous Goods shipment.The authorization field is only required to state special governmental authorizations that are required because of the provisions or packing materials shipped with.

fields.name: CHEMICAL_FORMULA
fields.description: The basic chemical description of the substance

fields.name: CONCENTRATION_PERCENT
fields.description: Required if the Dangerous Goods UN Number is 2031, 1796, 1826, or 1873. Enter the percentage of DG concentration if the above condition applies.

fields.name: CRIT_SAFETY_INDEX
fields.description: A measurement for packages containing fissile material that is determined by dividing the number 50 by the smaller of the numbers determined using the rules in section 10.6.2.4.3 of the DG Regulation Guide

fields.name: IDG_ADD_DESC_INFO
fields.description: Hazmat IDG additional description information

fields.name: MAX_QUANTITY
fields.description: Maximum passenger volume or the Maximum cargo volume for the item (Used in Q- Value calculation)

fields.name: OUTER_PACKAGING_TYPE
fields.description: Outer packaging type if packing type = All packed in one

fields.name: OUTER_PACKING_COUNT
fields.description: Outer Packing count

fields.name: IS_OVERPACK
fields.description: Flag to indicate the pkg is an 'overpack'

fields.name: PACKAGE_TYPE
fields.description: Hazmat Package type of the Dangerous Goods shipment.

fields.name: PACKING_COUNT
fields.description: Packing Count (number of packaged units)

fields.name: PACKING_INSTRUCTIONS
fields.description: Required for dangerous goods shipments, unless the DG class is radioactive or Not Restricted.See Table 4.2 columns G, I, or K of the IATA DG Regulations.

fields.name: PHYSICAL_FORM
fields.description: A description of the physical form of the radioactive substance : S(p)ecial (S)olid (L)iquid (G)as

fields.name: Q_VALUE
fields.description: Ratio used to indicate that the combined quantities in 'all packed in one' package are within the maximum allowable limits.

fields.name: HAZ_QUANTITY
fields.description: Hazmat item quantity

fields.name: RADIOACTIVE_LABEL
fields.description: The type of label on the shipment. W1 (White I) Y2 (Yellow II) Y3 (Yellow III)

fields.name: RAD_PACKAGING
fields.description: A description of the type of packaging the substance is contained in. Acceptable package types are listed in section 10.5 of the DG Regulations Guide: Expected ( Expected ) Industrial ip1 ( Industrial Type 1(IP-1) ) Industrial ip2 ( Industrial Type 2(IP-2) ) Industrial ip3 ( Industrial Type 3(IP-3) ) Type a ( Type A ) Type b(m) ( Type B(M) ) Type b(u) ( Type B(U) ) Type c ( Type C )

fields.name: RADIOACTIVITY
fields.description: A measurement of the activity of the shipment. For fissile material, the weight of the material in grams or kilograms may be substituted for the activity measurement

fields.name: RADIOACTIVITY_UNITS
fields.description: The units that the activity were mesaured in. This must be in becquerel units (or multiples thereof) : Bq (becquerel) kBq (kilobecquerel) MBq (megabecquerel) GBq (gigabecquerel) TBq (terabecquerel) PBq (petabecquerel)

fields.name: RADIONUCLIDE
fields.description: The radionuclide assigned to the substance being shipped. A table of basic radionuclides is available in the DG Regulation Guide section 10.4.A. For example, Aluminium (13) has a radionuclide Al-26

fields.name: SUBSTANCE_NUMBER
fields.description: Hazardous Materials substance number.

fields.name: SURFACE_READING
fields.description: The surface reading of the radiation exposure for the package. This reading must be measured in mrem/h

fields.name: TRANSPORT_INDEX
fields.description: A number based on the radiation exposure of a package. This is determined based on the maximum measurement of the radiation in mrem/h at a distance of 1 meter from the package surface

fields.name: MATERIALS_TYPE
fields.description: Hazardous Materials Type. Valid values are: l = Limited Quantities e = 500 KG Exemption f = Fully Regulated (Purolater)

fields.name: UNITS
fields.description: IATA units for Non UPS shipments (L=>Liters g=>Grams kg=>Kilograms kg G=>Kilograms Gross).

fields.name: EXTERNAL_CODE
fields.description: Item Hazmat code as configured in the TNT EM. (For carrier TNT)

tables.description: Basic Hours of Service rule. Building block for creating HOS Rule Set Option.

fields.name: HOS_ACTIVITY_TYPE
fields.description: Activity type corresponding to activity time is: D-drive, W-work, or R-rest.

fields.name: IS_ACTIVITY_TIME_MAX
fields.description: Y- Activity time is a maximum time.N- Activity time is a minimum.

fields.name: ALLOW_REST
fields.description: Y - Rest duration will be inserted when max activity time is reached. N - If max activity time is exceeded, rule will be in violation. No rest duration is inserted.

fields.name: IS_ACTIVITY_TIME_CONTIGUOUS
fields.description: Only applicable if IS_ACTIVITY_TIME_MAX is 'N'.

fields.name: IS_REST_TRIGGERED_BY_WORK
fields.description: Y- rest duration can be triggered during work activity or drive activity. N- rest duration can only be triggered during drive activity.

fields.name: MIN_RESET_SEPARATION
fields.description: Minimum duration required between the start of two resets

fields.name: RESET_START_WINDOW_BEGIN
fields.description: Earliest time of day that counting can start for a reset break. Time is stored in milliseconds.

fields.name: RESET_START_WINDOW_END
fields.description: Latest time of day that counting can start for a reset break. Time is stored in milliseconds.

tables.description: This table stores the standard incoterms used in the transportation industry. The incoterms are associated with an order or shipment. Incoterms are the internationally accepted definitions for terms of sale legislated by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC). The Incoterms further assign each party their respective performance responsibilities, elements of cost, and risks. The inclusion of incoterms in a sales agreement commits the buyer and seller to a strict interpretation of the standard definitions. Some examples of incoterms include: EXW EX works, FAS - Free alongside ship FOB - Free on board vessel, CFR - Cost and Freight, CIF - Cost, Insurance and Freight, DES - Delivered ex ship, DEQ - Delivered ex quay (duty paid) etc.

tables.description: One record per invoice. Main invoice table. Currently, an invoice relates to billing of one shipment, but in the future, multiple shipments may be consolidated onto an invoice. GC3 can populate this table from a shipment, or the invoice information can be fed into the system directly. An invoice is a relative term - a bill and an invoice look the same, but the person who receives it ("BILL TO" involved party) would consider it an invoice and the person generating it ("SERVICE PROVIDER" party) would consider it a bill.

fields.name: INVOICE_TYPE
fields.description: INVOICE_TYPE indicates whether this is a bill or an invoice (valid values are 'I' or 'B').INVOICE_TYPE indicates whether this is a bill or an invoice (valid values are 'I' or 'B').

tables.description: INVOICE_LINEITEM_REFNUM lists the reference number for each line item on an invoice (one record per reference number). fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: Qualified remarks about the invoice (qualifiers in table REMARK_QUAL). This may have similar information to the INVOICE_NOTE table, except that the remarks are qualified, while notes are freeform. Example: Qual: Decline reason Remark: Equipment not available.

tables.description: One record is created for every carrier (related to the shipment included on the invoice) per invoice. tables.description: Invoice_Split_Rule table contains the rules which are used to split Invoices/Bills into multiple invoices or bills for a single shipment. Invoice_Split_Rule table will define one of the valid ways that we can look at a shipment (buy or sell) and generate multiple bills or invoices from it based on the split rule that is being used.

fields.name: INVOICE_SPLIT_RULE_GID

fields.name: INVOICE_SPLIT_RULE_DESC
fields.description: INVOICE_SPLIT_RULE_DESC contains the description about split rule.

fields.name: INVOICE_SPLIT_RULE_TYPE
fields.description: INVOICE_SPLIT_RULE_TYPE indicates whether this RULE is applicable for a bill or an invoice (valid values are I or B)

fields.name: INVOICE_SPLIT_RULE_CRITERIA
fields.description: invoice_split_rule_criteria will define the criteria which is base for splitting the invoice. Valid Inputs are:L,T,A,S; L represents - PER COST LINE, T represents - PER COST TYPE, A represents - PER ACCESORIAL.

tables.description: One record per status per invoice. Contains the statuses of an invoice. For example, whether the invoice was issued, approved, sent, etc.

fields.name: STATUS_VALUE_GID
fields.description: STATUS_VALUE_GID contains the unique identifier for the status value on the invoice.

tables.description: Contains the stops that are related to the shipment referred to on the invoice. The difference between an invoice stop and an invoice route is that a new route indicates a change in transportation carrier (and the location of the interchange), while a stop is simply a stop to pick up or deliver goods.

fields.name: INVOICE_GID
fields.description: INVOICE_GID contains the unique identifier for the invoice.

fields.name: STOP_SEQ_NO
fields.description: Stop sequence number.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID

fields.name: LOCATION_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: LOCATION_REFNUM
fields.description: LOCATION_REFNUM contains a reference number for the location on the invoice.

fields.name: INVOICE_STOP_REASON_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: EQUIPMENT_PREFIX

fields.name: EQUIPMENT_NUMBER
fields.description: EQUIPMENT_NUMBER contains the equipment number on the invoice.

fields.name: START_DATE
fields.description: Start date.

fields.name: END_DATE
fields.description: End date.

fields.name: DOMAIN_NAME
fields.description: Domain of the User. Is used for creating the GID.

tables.description: Indicates the reason for shipment stops associated with the invoice. For example, "PICKUP", "DELIVERY", etc.

fields.name: INVOICE_STOP_REASON_DESC

tables.description: Qualified reference information that relates to the stop itself. Example:PO or SO number.

fields.name: STOP_SEQ_NO

tables.description: INVOICE_STOP_SEAL contains the serial number of the security device that relates to the stop.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the qualifier can exist on a business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: This is the item master table. It defines the various items in the customer"s database. The item can be defined in various governmental classification categories. GC3 includes the categories to represent NMFC, STCC, HTS and SITC. Other details like, minimum, maximum shipping units, pallet size etc. are also defined in this table.

fields.description: Attribute fields (flexfields) allow you to add additional information about an object. Data Type NUMBER

fields.name: ATTRIBUTE_NUMBER10
fields.description: Attribute fields (flexfields) allow you to add additional information about an object. Data Type NUMBER

fields.name: IS_VISIBLE_IF_MASTER_CARR
fields.description: Identifies whether the reference numbers with this qualifier should display on related shipments.

fields.name: DEFAULT_REFNUM_BN_TYPE_GID
fields.description: The BN type used to generate a refnum when the refnum field for a record in the corresponding refnum table is not populated.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: Connects items together.
This table contains any free entry text remarks for the item, that the user wants to add.

fields.name: ITEM_GID
fields.description: ITEM_GID contains the unique identifier for the item being shipped.

fields.name: REMARK_SEQUENCE
fields.description: REMARK_SEQUENCE contains the sequence number for the item remark.

fields.name: REMARK_QUAL_GID
fields.description: REMARK_QUAL_GID contains the unique identifier for the remark qualifier.

fields.name: REMARK_TEXT
fields.description: REMARK_TEXT contains the text of the remark about the item.

fields.name: DOCUMENT_CMS_NAME
fields.description: The document name used to identify a set of revisions (versions) for a CMS.

tables.description: This table has a link to the special services table. It defines the special services required to ship or deliver an item. The special services differ from the accessories, in that the customer does not have to pay for the special services.

fields.name: ITEM_GID
fields.description: ITEM_GID contains the unique identifier for the item being shipped.

fields.name: SPECIAL_SERVICE_GID
fields.description: SPECIAL_SERVICE_GID contains the unique identifier for the special service required for the item being shipped.

tables.description: The item_text table associates an item to a particular set of text. An item can have more than one set of text, and the text can be shared by more than one item.

fields.name: DOCUMENT_DEF_GID
fields.description: Document that should hold text

fields.name: TEXT_OVERRIDE
fields.description: Transactional override for the template on the specific document

tables.description: This table defines the path between two locations. The itinerary is described in terms of the legs. An itinerary can have one or more legs. The itinerary table consists of the constraints on the weight allowed for shipment for each path. The itinerary can be used by a corporation or can specify additional constraints using the calendar GID like Monday-Friday Itinerary, Weekend Itinerary etc.

fields.name: ITINERARY_GID
fields.description: ITINERARY_GID contains the unique identifier for the intinerary.

fields.name: ITINERARY_XID
fields.description: ITINERARY_XID contains the unique external identifier for the itinerary.

fields.name: ITINERARY_NAME
fields.description: ITINERARY_NAME contains the name for the itinerary.

fields.name: DESCRIPTION
fields.description: DESCRIPTION contains text describing the itinerary.

fields.name: MIN_WEIGHT
fields.description: MIN_WEIGHT contains the minimum weight for the shipment on the itinerary.

fields.name: MIN_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MIN_WEIGHT column.

fields.name: MIN_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: MIN_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: MAX_WEIGHT
fields.description: MAX_WEIGHT contains the maximum weight for the shipment on the itinerary.

fields.name: MAX_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MAX_WEIGHT column.

fields.name: MAX_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: MAX_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: MIN_VOLUME
fields.description: MIN_VOLUME contains the minimum volume for the shipment on the itinerary.

fields.name: MIN_VOLUME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MIN_VOLUME column.

fields.name: MIN_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: MIN_VOLUME in base units.

fields.name: MAX_VOLUME
fields.description: MAX_VOLUME contains the maximum volume for the shipment on the itinerary.

fields.name: MAX_VOLUME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MAX_VOLUME column.

fields.name: MAX_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: MAX_VOLUME in base units.

fields.name: CORPORATION_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: CORPORATION_PROFILE_GID contains the unique identifier for the corporation profile used for the itinerary.

fields.name: CALENDAR_GID
fields.description: CALENDAR_GID contains the unique identifier for the calendar.

fields.name: X_LANE_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: IS_MULTI_STOP
fields.description: IS_MULTI_STOP describes whether the itinerary contains more than one stop. IF so, this column contains a Y for yes; if not it contains an N for no.

fields.name: TOTAL_COST_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: TOTAL_COST_BASE
fields.description: TOTAL_COST in base units.

fields.name: TOTAL_TRANSIT_TIME_BASE
fields.description: TOTAL_TRANSIT_TIME in base units.

fields.name: USE_DECONSOLIDATION_POOL
fields.description: Orders matched to this itinerary may use a de-consolidation pool on the destination.

fields.name: USE_CONSOLIDATION_POOL
fields.description: : Orders matched to this itinerary may use a consolidation pool on the src.

fields.name: MIN_STOPOFF_WEIGHT
fields.description: order bundles less than this weight will always go through a pool if the orgin/dest of the order bundles fall into consolidation/de-consolidation pools

fields.name: MIN_STOPOFF_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MIN_STOPOFF_WEIGHT column.

fields.name: MIN_STOPOFF_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: MIN_STOPOFF_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: MAX_POOL_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: MAX_POOL_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: TOTAL_STOPS_CONSTRAINT
fields.description: : it specifies the maximum number of stops on a multi-stop shipment derived from this itinerary.

fields.name: MIN_TL_WEIGHT
fields.description: : This is the minimum weight to make up a nice truckload. It is also used as the minimum weight to go through a pool

fields.name: MIN_TL_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MIN_TL_WEIGHT column.

fields.name: MIN_TL_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: MIN_TL_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: MIN_STOPOFF_VOLUME
fields.description: Order bundles less than this volume will always go through a pool if the origin/dest of the order bundles fall into consolidation/de-consolidation pools. When doing pool drop swapping, only pool drop shipments whose volume is bigger than this value are tried.

fields.name: MIN_STOPOFF_VOLUME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for the min_stopoff_volume.

fields.name: MIN_STOPOFF_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: Stopoff volume in base units.

fields.name: MAX_POOL_VOLUME
fields.description: This is the maximum volume to go through a pool.

fields.name: MAX_POOL_VOLUME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for the maximum pool volume.

fields.name: MAX_POOL_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: Maximum volume to go through a pool in base units.

fields.name: MAX_SMALL_DIRECT_VOLUME
fields.description: After pool swapping, we try to pull off the orders that are close to parcel weight and ship direct, if the overall solution is cheaper. This volume guides the algorithm to only pull of the one that is smaller than this volume.

fields.name: MAX_SMALL_DIRECT_VOL_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for the small direct volume.

fields.name: MAX_SMALL_DIRECT_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: Small direct volume in base units.

fields.name: MAX_SMALL_DIRECT_WEIGHT
fields.description: After pool swapping, we try to pull off the orders that are close to parcel weight and ship direct, if the overall solution is cheaper. This weight guides the algorithm to only pull of the one that is smaller than this weight.

fields.name: MAX_SMALL_DIRECT_WGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for small direct weight.

fields.name: MAX_SMALL_DIRECT_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: Small direct weight in base units.

fields.name: MIN_TL_USAGE_PERCENTAGE
fields.description: The percentage of usage to be used to determine whether a TL is full in bulk plan. This will guide whether direct shipment be kept without routing to pool. It also determines whether pool shipments be kept without routing to xdock.

fields.name: IS_DEST_BUNDLE_PREFERED
fields.description: If this flag is set, when going through the de-consolidation pool process, if there is a x-dock on this itinerary, two or more order bundles have the same destination will not route to the de-consolidation pool. It will route to x-dock later for consolidation at x-dock and direct go to the destination

fields.name: HAZMAT_MODE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the transportation modes. (includes the domain name as part of the key)

fields.name: ITINERARY_TYPE
fields.description: When 'N', indicates itinerary is inactive and should not be used by the algorithm.

fields.name: PICKUP_STOPS_CONSTRAINTS
fields.description: total # of pickup stops for a multistop shipment. It is usually set to be the max of PICKUP_STOPS_CONSTRAINTS of all valid rate offering. This help multistop performance

fields.name: DELIVERY_STOPS_CONSTRAINTS
fields.description: total # of pickup stops for a multistop shipment. It is usually set to be the max of DELIVERY_STOPS_CONSTRAINTS of all valid rate offering. This help multistop performance

fields.name: MAX_DIST_BTW_PICKUPS
fields.description: if distance between 2 pickup stops is beyond this, don't pair 2 shipments in multistop

fields.name: RADIUS_FOR_PICKUPS
fields.description: the distance from any pickup stop to the root bundle's source location can not exceed this field.

fields.name: RADIUS_PERCENTAGE_FOR_PICKUPS
fields.description: distance from any pickup to the root source divided by the root distance can not exceed this planning parameter.

fields.name: PAYMENT_METHOD_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: List of compatible payment methods for the itinerary

tables.description: This table joins the Itinerary table to the Leg table. It is used to resolve the many-to-many relationship between Itinerary and Leg.

fields.name: ITINERARY_GID
fields.description: ITINERARY_GID contains the unique identifier for the itinerarty.

fields.name: LEG_GID
fields.description: LEG_GID contains the unique identifier for a leg on the itinerary.

tables.description: This table contains the audit trail information about the XML transmissions and the transactions within them. The status of the transmission and transactions received, processed or in error is stored per record. The status is stored as I-Information, W-Warning, E-Errors. In other words: The I_LOG table store integration log messages, which include errors, warnings, and informational messages.

fields.name: I_LOG_SEQNO
fields.description: The I_LOG_SEQNO column uniquely identifies each row in the I_LOG table.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: The I_TRANSACTION_NO column specifies the transaction to which this log message pertains.

fields.name: I_TRANSMISSION_NO
fields.description: The I_TRANSMISSION_NO column specifies the transmission to which this log message pertains.

fields.name: WRITTEN_BY
fields.description: The WRITTEN_BY column specifies the application that wrote this log message.

fields.name: I_MESSAGE_CLASS
fields.description: The I_MESSAGE_CLASS column indicates if the message is an error (E), warning (W) or informational (I) in nature.

fields.name: I_MESSAGE_CODE
fields.description: The I_MESSAGE_CODE column is a textual message code. An example is INVALID_DATE_FORMAT.

fields.name: I_MESSAGE_TEXT
fields.description: The I_MESSAGE_TEXT column contains the full text of the message.

fields.name: TIME_STAMP
fields.description: The TIME_STAMP column indicates the date and time for when the log message was written.

fields.name: PARTITION_KEY
fields.description: Populated through a trigger, this key is used to determine into which partition this record should be inserted. The formula is based upon the insert_date column and the frequency in which this table is associated (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Valid values are 1-4.

fields.name: LOG_PROCESS_ID
fields.description: Process ID of process which inserted the log records

tables.description: This table is related to the I_LOG table for storing the details of the audit trail information for the XML transmissions and transactions. The I_LOG_DETAIL table stores qualifier/value pairs associated with a given log message.

fields.name: I_LOG_DETAIL_SEQNO
fields.description: I_LOG_DETAIL_SEQNO provides a sequence number for a transaction.

fields.name: I_LOG_SEQNO
fields.description: A pointer back to the log sequence number from I_Log table.

fields.name: I_LOG_DETAIL_QUAL
fields.description: I_LOG_DETAIL_QUAL provides the application name, transaction code and transaction number for an XML transaction.

fields.name: I_LOG_DETAIL_VALUE
fields.description: The actual value for log detail.

fields.name: PARTITION_KEY
fields.description: Populated through a trigger, this key is used to determine into which partition this record should be inserted. The formula is based upon the insert_date column and the frequency in which this table is associated (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Valid values are 1-4.

tables.description: The I_TRANSACTION table store all XML transactions which are received by GLog, or sent out of GLog. The I_TRANSACTION table is a child of the I_LOG_TRANSMISSION table. This table contains the one record per transaction in the XML transmissions. See table I_TRANSMISSION for the XML transmission details. Each transaction within the transmission are broken out and stored as individual records.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: This is a foreign key from the I_LOG table. The I_TRANSACTION_NO column specifies the transaction to which this log message pertains.

fields.name: I_TRANSMISSION_NO
fields.description: This is a foreign key to the I_TRANSMISSION table. The I_TRANSMISSION_NO column uniquely identifies the transmission.

fields.name: TRANSACTION_CODE
fields.description: TRANSACTION_CODE provides a code for the type of transaction that was transmitted. Transaction codes include: D I IU U Unknown

fields.name: WRITTEN_BY
fields.description: WRITTEN_BY contains the domain name that logged the transmission.

fields.name: STATUS
fields.description: STATUS indicates the status of the transaction. Status types include: FRESH New PROCESSED

fields.name: ELEMENT_NAME
fields.description: ELEMENT_NAME contains the name of the transaction element. These can include: ActualShipment AllocationBase Billing Invoice Item Location Rate Release RemoteQuery ShipmentStatus TenderOffer TenderResponse TransOrder Voucher Xlane

fields.name: SENDER_TRANSACTION_ID
fields.description: Specifies the Transaction Number that the sender assigned to the transaction.

fields.name: REFERENCE_TRANSACTION_ID
fields.description: Specifies an alternative Transaction Number that may be assigned by the recipient for the transaction.

fields.name: EXTERNAL_STATUS
fields.description: Specifies the external status for the Transaction. The external status represents the result of processing at the external system.

fields.name: TRANSACTION_TYPE
fields.description: Specifies the type of information stored in the Transmission record. For example, a record may be stored when an XML is staged via OAQ to support searching via Integration Saved Queries.

fields.name: DATA_QUERY_TYPE_GID
fields.description: Data query type of OBJECT_GID, if populated.

fields.name: XML_BLOB
fields.description: The XML_BLOB column specifies the entire XML text of the transmission.

fields.name: PARTITION_KEY
fields.description: Populated through a trigger, this key is used to determine into which partition this record should be inserted. The formula is based upon the insert_date column and the frequency in which this table is associated (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Valid values are 1-4.

tables.description: The table contains the Transaction Ack that are received for outbound Transmissions.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: The Transaction numer referring to the Transaction for the Ack.

fields.name: SEQUENCE_NO
fields.description: Sequence number to uniquely identify the Transaction Ack for the Transaction.

fields.name: ACK_STATUS
fields.description: Specifies the status provided in the Transaction Ack.

fields.name: ACK_REASON
fields.description: Specifies the reason for the status provided within the Transaction Ack.

fields.name: REF_TRANSACTION_ID
fields.description: Represents a Transaction ID assigned for the Transaction from the external system.

fields.name: REF_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: Represents the Transaction record containing the xml for this Transaction Ack.

fields.name: PARTITION_KEY
fields.description: Populated through a trigger, this key is used to determine into which partition this record should be inserted. The formula is based upon the insert_date column and the frequency in which this table is associated (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Valid values are 1-4.

tables.description: The I_TRANSACTION_DETAIL table provides additional details for the I_TRANSACTION when the Transaction applies to more than one object gid.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: Transaction Number.

fields.name: SEQUENCE_NO
fields.description: Sequence number.

fields.name: STATUS
fields.description: Specifies the status of the transaction for the object gid.

fields.name: OBJECT_GID
fields.description: Specifies the Gid of the Object that was affected by the Transaction.

fields.name: NEW_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: If the processing of the object required obtaining a new i_transaction_no, this field provides a reference to the number. Note: An i_transaction record may not exist for this new transaction.

fields.name: OBJECT_SEQUENCE
fields.description: Specifies the sequence number for the primary key for the object.

tables.description: Transaction reference numbers.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: Transaction number uniquely defines the Transaction.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: Transaction reference number qualifier.

fields.name: REFNUM_VALUE
fields.description: Reference number value.

fields.name: PARTITION_KEY
fields.description: Populated through a trigger, this key is used to determine into which partition this record should be inserted. The formula is based upon the insert_date column and the frequency in which this table is associated (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Valid values are 1-4.

tables.description: Qualifiers for Transaction reference numbers

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: Transaction reference number qualifier gid.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_REFNUM_QUAL_XID
fields.description: Transaction reference number qualifier xid.

fields.name: DESCRIPTION
fields.description: Description of the reference qualifier.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: The I_TRANSMISSION table stores all XML transmissions which are received by GLog, or sent out of Glog. This table is an audit trail for the XML transmissions sent to the GC3 system. These transmissions can be query transmissions or may contain one or more transactions to be carried out by GC3. The actual transmission is stored as a LONG variable. The details abut the user sending the transmission are also stored.

fields.name: I_TRANSMISSION_NO
fields.description: The I_TRANSMISSION_NO column uniquely identifies the transmission.

fields.name: CREATE_DATE
fields.description: The CREATE_DATE column specifies when the transmission was first created.

fields.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
fields.description: The TRANSACTION_COUNT column specifies the number of transactions associated with this transmission.

fields.name: SENDER_HOST_NAME
fields.description: The SENDER_HOST_NAME column specifies the name of the machine that sent the transmission.

fields.name: RECEIVER_HOST_NAME
fields.description: The SENDER_HOST_NAME column specifies the name of the machine that received the transmission.

fields.name: USERNAME
fields.description: The USERNAME column specifies the GLog username used to log into GLog to persist the transmission. This is required when IS_INBOUND is Y.

fields.name: PASSWORD
fields.description: The PASSWORD column specifies the GLog password used to log into GLog to persist the transmission. This is required when IS_INBOUND is Y.

fields.name: IS_INBOUND
fields.description: The IS_INBOUND column indicates that a transmission is either inbound (value equals Y) or not (value equals N).

fields.name: SENDER_TRANSMISSION_NO
fields.description: The SENDER_TRANSMISSION_NO column specifies the transmission number that the sender assigned to the transmission.

fields.name: REFERENCE_TRANSMISSION_NO
fields.description: The REFERENCE_TRANSMISSION_NO column can be used when GLog responds to a prior transmission. In this case, it specifies to which transmission the response belongs.

fields.name: STATUS
fields.description: The STATUS column is set to FRESH when the transmission is first stored in the I_TRANSMISSION table. Currently, it is never being changed to anything else.

fields.name: ACK_COM_METHOD_GID
fields.description: The ACK_COM_METHOD_GID column may be set to EMAIL to request email acknowledgement of a transmission.

fields.name: ACK_EMAIL_ADDRESS
fields.description: The ACK_EMAIL_ADDRESS column holds the valid email address to which a transmission acknowledgement gets sent if email is set in the ACK_COM_METHOD_GID column.

fields.name: ACK_EMAIL_SMTPHOST
fields.description: The ACK_EMAIL_SMTPHOST column specifies the hostname to send an email acknowledegment of receipt of the transmission.

fields.name: ACK_SERVLET_URL
fields.description: The URL for sending teh acknowledgement.

fields.name: XML_BLOB
fields.description: The XML_BLOB column specifies the entire XML text of the transmission.

fields.name: XML_PI
fields.description: This field contains the XML Processing Instructions.

fields.name: EXT_SYS_COM_METHOD_GID
fields.description: Indicates the communication method used for outbound XML Transmissions.

fields.name: EXT_SYS_COM_INFO
fields.description: This field specifies the attributes of the external system communication method used for the outbound integration. For example, when the comm method is FTP, this column would contain the filename.

fields.name: PARTITION_KEY
fields.description: Populated through a trigger, this key is used to determine into which partition this record should be inserted. The formula is based upon the insert_date column and the frequency in which this table is associated (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Valid values are 1-4.

fields.name: STOP_PROCESS_ON_ERROR
fields.description: Specifies if processing of the Transmission should be stopped when an error occurs in a Transaction.

fields.name: LOG_PROCESS_ID
fields.description: Process ID for the Transmission.

fields.name: XML_BLOB_TYPE
fields.description: Specifies if the contents of the XML_BLOB is the complete Transmission XML(T), the Transmission Header(TH), Message XML(M), Message Header XML(MH), Message Body XML(MB) or is not used (NA).

fields.name: ADD_XML_DECLARATION
fields.description: Specifies if the XML declaration should be added when sending the XML.

fields.name: ADD_TRANSMISSION_WRAPPER
fields.description: Specifies if the Transmission XML element should be used to wrap the individual Transaction elements.

fields.name: TRANSMISSION_WRAPPER_ELEMENT
fields.description: Specifies the element name to wrap the Transmission.

fields.name: XML_BLOB_SIZE
fields.description: Specifies the size of the XML.

fields.name: EXTERNAL_STATUS
fields.description: Specifies the external status for the Transmission. The external status represents the result of processing at the external system.

fields.name: FIRST_ATTEMPT_DATE
fields.description: Specifies the first time an attempt is made to process the transmission.

fields.name: LAST_ATTEMPT_DATE
fields.description: Specifies the last time an attempt is made to process the transmission.

fields.name: MIME_TYPE
fields.description: Describes the XML_BLOB content.

fields.name: VERSION
fields.description: Specifies the app version which corresponds to the XML_BLOB.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: The leg defines the path between the source and destination. It identifies other constraints like mode of transportation (only trucks allowed, air mode etc.)

fields.description: Flag indicating how equipment groups are assigned for this Leg. A value of 'N' indicates that No Equipment will be used (i.e. it's loose freight), 'O' stands for Optimize Equipment (i.e. run through Container Optimization), and 'R' stands for Re-use Equipment from Previous Leg (or Optimize if no previous equipment is available).

fields.name: RATE_OFFERING_GID
