fields.name: RULE_11_INDICATOR
fields.description: If not null Identifies the Leg as being part of a rail rule_11 Itinerary segment and determines if the shipment built from the Leg is tendered or not.

fields.name: RAIL_INTER_MODAL_PLAN_GID
fields.description: This is the rail inter-modal plan gid. I.e.) Plan 20 - door-to-door, Plan 25 - ramp-to-ramp, etc.

fields.name: CUSTOMER_RATE_CODE
fields.description: This code signifies what rate is used on a Leg, either 'C' for the Customer's rate, or 'S' for the Shipper's rate.

fields.name: COFC_TOFC
fields.description: Container-On-Flat-Car_Trailer-On-Flat-Car code used along with the Rail_Inter_Modal_Plan_gid to determine the rate.

fields.name: SERVPROV_ASSIGNMENT_TYPE
fields.description: Valid values are O and R, which stand for Optimize and Re-use. "Optimize" will be the default and allow GC3 to work the way it does currently where it finds the best service provider for each leg. "Re-use" will force GC3 to keep the same service provider on this leg that was used on the previous one.

tables.description: Tolerance thresholds for individual shipment cost/invoice lines. For given shipment cost/invoice line attributes such as cost type and accessorial code, users may specify tolerance thresholds to prevent auto approval of invoice lines that are too much higer or lower than the corresponding shipment cost.

fields.name: LINE_APPROVE_TOLERANCE_GID
fields.description: ID for the line approval tolerance rule

fields.name: IS_INVOICE_REFNUM_MATCH_ALL
fields.description: If true, all invoice reference numbers in the LINE_APP_TOL_INVOICE_REFNUM table must exist on the invoice to evaluate this rule; if false, only one must exist

fields.name: IS_SHIPMENT_REFNUM_MATCH_ALL
fields.description: If true, all shipment reference numbers in the LINE_APP_TOL_SHIPMENT_REFNUM table must exist on the shipment to evaluate this rule; if false, only one must exist

fields.name: EFFECTIVE_DATE
fields.description: The Effective Date for this Line Approval Tolerance Rule.

fields.name: EXPIRATION_DATE
fields.description: The Expiration Date for this Line Approval Tolerance Rule.

tables.description: Individual tolerance settings for the given shipment cost/invoice line attributes; for example, all accessorial costs on the invoice line with accessorial code FUEL SURCHARGE may be 5% above the corresponding shipment cost.

fields.name: COST_TYPE
fields.description: Optional; indicates the cost type to apply tolerance checks between shipment cost and invoice line

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID
fields.description: Optional; indicates the accessorial code to apply tolerance checks between shipment cost and invoice line

fields.name: GENERAL_LEDGER_GID
fields.description: Optional; indicates the general ledger code to apply tolerance checks between shipment cost and invoice line

fields.name: PAYMENT_METHOD_CODE_GID
fields.description: Optional; indicates the payment method code to apply tolerance checks between shipment cost and invoice line

fields.name: ALLOWABLE_PERCENT_ABOVE
fields.description: For the given cost attributes, the allowable percentage the invoice line may be above the corresponding shipment cost

fields.name: ALLOW_CURR_ABOVE
fields.description: For the given cost attributes, the allowable monetary amount the invoice line may be above the corresponding shipment cost

fields.name: ALLOWABLE_PERCENT_BELOW
fields.description: For the given cost attributes, the allowable percentage the invoice line may be below the corresponding shipment cost

fields.name: ALLOW_CURR_BELOW
fields.description: For the given cost attributes, the allowable monetary amount the invoice line may be below the corresponding shipment cost

tables.description: Defines various valid load configurations, OSR, orientation, stacktability and row restriction.

fields.name: LOAD_CONFIG_GID
fields.description: Load configuration id.

fields.name: NUM_STACKING_LAYERS
fields.description: Number of stacking layers. Null implies no restriction.

fields.name: NUM_LOADING_ROWS
fields.description: Number of loading rows. Null implies no restriction.

tables.description: Defined load configuration engine types available in GC3.

fields.name: STACKING_INDEX
fields.description: Stacking index of the ship unit spec. Units with higher stacking index should not be loaded on top of units with lower stacking index.

fields.name: MAX_STACKING_HEIGHT
fields.description: Defines the height this unit cannot be stacked above since unloading would be difficult otherwise.

fields.name: MAX_STACKING_LAYERS
fields.description: Number of layers this unit can be stacked all by itself.

fields.name: IS_STACKABLE_ABOVE
fields.description: To indicate if anything can be stacked on top of it.

fields.name: IS_STACKABLE_BELOW
fields.description: To indicate if anything can be stacked below it.

fields.name: STACKING_RULE
fields.description: This is an INCLUSIVE rule. SAME means SAME items can be stacked together. LIKE means SAME or LIKE items can be stacked together. Null means any items can be stacked together.

fields.name: MAX_TOP_WEIGHT
fields.description: Maximum allowable weight can be loaded on top of this type of unit when loaded with the orientation. This is only checked for non-like units. It does not apply when stacking like units.

fields.name: MAX_LENGTH_OVERHANG
fields.description: When loaded in the given orientation, maximum unsupported overhang along the length of the unit itself.

fields.name: MAX_WIDTH_OVERHANG
fields.description: When loaded in the given orientation, maximum unsupported overhang along the width of the unit itself.

tables.description: A Location is a place where transportation related activities occur, such as loading and unloading freight. In addition, a location may represent a corporation, and/or a service provider.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the location.

fields.name: LOCATION_XID
fields.description: LOCATION_XID contains the unique external identifier for the location.

fields.name: LOCATION_NAME
fields.description: LOCATION_NAME contains the name of the location.

fields.name: CITY
fields.description: CITY contains the name of the city for the location.

fields.name: PROVINCE
fields.description: PROVINCE contains the province (or U.S. state) for the location.

fields.name: PROVINCE_CODE
fields.description: PROVINCE_CODE contains the abbreviation for the provice (or U.S. state) for the location.

fields.name: POSTAL_CODE
fields.description: POSTAL_CODE contains the postal code for the location.

fields.name: COUNTRY_CODE3_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the three digit country code in GC3.

fields.name: ZONE1
fields.description: The zone where the location fits in.

fields.name: ZONE2
fields.description: The zone where the location fits in.

fields.name: ZONE3
fields.description: The zone where the location fits in.

fields.name: ZONE4
fields.description: The zone where the location fits in.

fields.name: TIME_ZONE_GID
fields.description: TIME_ZONE_GID contains the unique identifier for the time zone of the location. Examples of time zones include America/Curacao and Europe/Monaco.

fields.name: LAT
fields.description: LAT contains the latitude for the location.

fields.name: LON
fields.description: LON contains the longitude of the location.

fields.name: SOURCING_GROUP_NAME
fields.description: Another way of grouping the locations.

fields.name: DELIVERY_GROUP_NAME
fields.description: Another way of grouping the locations.

fields.name: REGION_GID
fields.description: REGION_GID contains the unique identifier for the region.

fields.name: SERVPROV_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: SERVPROV_PROFILE_GID contains the unique identifier for the service provider for the location.

fields.name: LOCATION_GROUP_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GROUP_GID contains the unique identifier for the location group for the location.

fields.name: IS_TEMPORARY
fields.description: A flag to identify if the location is created temporarily for a certain shipment or order. This location will not be used for other shipments or processes.

fields.name: IS_MAKE_APPT_BEFORE_PLAN
fields.description: A tru or false flag to identify if an appointment is required at the location for (example) for pickup, delivery etc.

fields.name: IS_ADDRESS_VALID
fields.description: Flag to indicate whether the address is considered valid or not (by checking against the POSTAL_CODE_VALIDATE table).

fields.name: RATE_CLASSIFICATION_GID
fields.description: RATE_CLASSIFICATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the rate classification. Examples include: COMMERCIAL and RESIDENTIAL.

fields.name: SLOT_TIME_INTERVAL
fields.description: The smallest appointment slot interval for the location.

fields.name: NUM_OF_ROWS_IN_YARD
fields.description: The total number of rows in the yard of the location.

fields.name: NUM_OF_SLOTS_PER_ROW_IN_YARD
fields.description: The number of slots per row in the yard of the location.

fields.name: USE_APPOINTMENT_PRIORITY
fields.description: Whether or not to consider appointment priority

fields.name: SCHEDULE_LOW_PRIORITY_APPOINT
fields.description: Whether or not to schedule appointment for low priority appointment that has been bumped off.

fields.name: ENFORCE_TIME_WINDOW_APPOINT
fields.description: Whether or not to enforce the pick up or delivery time window during appointment schedule process."USE_APPOINTMENT_PRIORITY" takes precedence of "ENFORCE_TIME_WINDOW_APPOINT".

fields.name: SCHEDULE_INFEASIBLE_APPOINT
fields.description: Whether or not to schedule appointment if the appointment time available is out of pick up or delivery time window.

fields.name: BB_MAX_CASES_PER_BATCH
fields.description: Maximum number of cases allowed in each batch. If blank, OTM will assume that there is no limit. If the number of cases in the order exceeds this maximum, then the batch balancer's splitting logic will distribute the cases into multiple batches uniformly while honoring certain rules.

fields.name: BB_IS_NEW_STORE
fields.description: Indicates if this location should be treated as a new store by the Batch Balancer logic. New stores can only be assigned by Batch Balancer to a DOOR whose LOAD_NEW_STORE field is set to Y.

fields.name: BB_ORDER_SPLIT_METHOD
fields.description: Value of "CONVEYABLE" causes only conveyable cases to be counted toward max cases, value of "ALL" causes all cases (including non-con and repack) to count toward max cases.

fields.name: EXCLUDE_FROM_ROUTE_EXECUTION

tables.description: The accessories required at a location for loading/ unloading the shipments. The accessories are different from the special services in that, the customer has to pay for the accessories.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the location.

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID
fields.description: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID contains the unique identifier for the accessorial code for the location.

tables.description: Location activity time definition table
This table defines location capacity constraint in terms of amount of capacity that can be handled in given time.

fields.name: CALENDAR_GID
fields.description: The calendar specifies the activities and times that a location capacity can be defined for.

fields.name: IS_ACTIVE
fields.description: This flag turns on/off the location capacity

fields.name: CAPACITY_TYPE
fields.description: Possible values: WEIGHT, VOLUME, SHIPMENT, TEU, FEU, and all Equipment_Reference_Unit_GIDs

fields.name: CAPACITY_ALLOCATION_RULE
fields.description: Possible values: FIRST BUCKET, ALL BUCKETS, DISTRIBUTE

fields.name: TIME_BUCKET_DURATION
fields.description: Duration of each bucket

fields.name: MAXIMUM_QUANTITY
fields.description: The maximum quantity per bucket the location can handle. Cannot be exceeded. Only valid when CAPACITY_TYPE is neither WEIGHT nor VOLUME.

fields.name: MAXIMUM_WEIGHT
fields.description: The maximum weight per bucket the location can handle. Cannot be exceeded. Only valid when CAPACITY_TYPE is WEIGHT.

fields.name: MAXIMUM_VOLUME
fields.description: The maximum volume per bucket the location can handle. Cannot be exceeded. Only valid when CAPACITY_TYPE is VOLUME.

fields.name: TARGET_QUANTITY
fields.description: The ideal quantity per bucket the location should handle. Can be exceeded, but pay above target penalty. Only valid when CAPACITY_TYPE is neither WEIGHT nor VOLUME.

fields.name: TARGET_WEIGHT
fields.description: The ideal weight per bucket the location should handle. Only valid when CAPACITY_TYPE is WEIGHT.

fields.name: TARGET_VOLUME
fields.description: The ideal volume per bucket the location should handle. Only valid when CAPACITY_TYPE is VOLUME.

fields.name: BELOW_TARGET_PENALTY
fields.description: Below target penalty per quantity, or per weight unit (TARGET_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE), or per volume unit (TARGET_VOLUME_UOM_CODE)

fields.name: ABOVE_TARGET_PENALTY
fields.description: Above target penalty per quantity, or per weight unit (TARGET_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE), or per volume unit (TARGET_VOLUME_UOM_CODE)

tables.description: This table specifies a group of location capacities to be used together and how they are triggered.

fields.name: TARGET_QUANTITY

fields.name: TARGET_VOLUME

tables.description: The compatibility information between two locations. For e.g., two competitive locations may not like to get their shipment via the same truck.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID1
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID2
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

tables.description: If a location is also a corporate headquarter, the information is stored in this table. Thus, the location gid will be in both location table and location_corporation table.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the location.

fields.name: CORPORATION_GID
fields.description: CORPORATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the location's corporation.

tables.description: Multiple locations can be grouped into a location group. This table stores the information about the groups.

fields.name: LOCATION_GROUP_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GROUP_GID contains the unique identifier for the location group.

fields.name: LOCATION_GROUP_XID
fields.description: LOCATION_GROUP_XID contains the unique external identifier for the location group.

fields.name: LOCATION_GROUP_NAME
fields.description: LOCATION_GROUP_NAME contains the name for the location group.

tables.description: This is similar to the Location_Compatibity table. Two location groups may not be compatible for a shipment via the same truck as an example. If the locations are grouped and the compatibility information is stored in the Location_Group_Compatibility table, it may reduce the number of entries for individual compatible records in the Location_Compatibility table.
Represents an actual load/unload point as it relates to a location along with data that is associated with that relationship.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: Location at which the load/unload point resides

fields.description: Load/unload point such as a loading dock

fields.name: EQUIPMENT_GROUP_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: Optional equipment group profile for the load/unload point; overrides any at the location

fields.name: IS_LOAD
fields.description: Indicates if this dock or point make be used for loading

fields.name: LOAD_SEQUENCE
fields.description: Optional, indicates where dock should be in a load sequence

fields.name: IS_UNLOAD
fields.description: Indicates if this dock or point make be used for loading

fields.name: UNLOAD_SEQUENCE
fields.description: Optional, indicates where dock should be in an unload sequence

tables.description: This is an alternate method of grouping the locations by creating enumerated profiles. This allows flexibility to group locations in a profile and specify if they are compatible for delivery.

fields.name: LOCATION_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_PROFILE_GID contains the unique identifier for the location's profile.

fields.name: LOCATION_PROFILE_XID
fields.description: LOCATION_PROFILE_XID contains the unique external identifier for the location profile.

fields.name: LOCATION_PROFILE_NAME
fields.description: LOCATION_PROFILE_NAME contains the name of the location profile.

tables.description: This table is used to resolve the many-to-many relationship between the Locations and the Location Profiles. A location can be part of one or more location profiles.

fields.name: LOCATION_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_PROFILE_GID contains the unique identifier for the location profile.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the location.

tables.description: A reference to a location among the various application systems within a company, which may be either a location global identifier or a new location definition. For example, a location may be referenced as XYZ in the ERP app, 123 in the JDE app etc within the same company.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: LOCATION_GID contains the unique identifier for the location.

fields.name: LOCATION_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

tables.description: A qualifier to the location reference. This table contains the actual identifier used by the company for the same location, between various systems (SAP, JDE etc.)

fields.name: LOCATION_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: LOCATION_REFNUM_QUAL_XID
fields.description: GC3 Unique identifier for the entity. Is used for creating the GID

fields.name: IS_VISIBLE_IF_MASTER_CARR
fields.description: Identifies whether the reference numbers with this qualifier should display on related shipments.

fields.name: DEFAULT_REFNUM_BN_TYPE_GID
fields.description: The BN type used to generate a refnum when the refnum field for a record in the corresponding refnum table is not populated.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: Additional information about the location. For e.g., address line3, 4 can be added to the location address via this table. A location can have many remarks associated with it.

tables.description: Long Running Task - provides visibility and control to long-running tasks

fields.name: TASK_SEQUENCE
fields.description: Unique sequence number for the LRT

fields.name: START_TIME
fields.description: Time the LRT was submitted. This may reflect the time it was placed in the workflow mediator or queues.

fields.name: END_TIME
fields.description: The time the LRT completed.

fields.name: STATE
fields.description: State of the LRT: MEDIATING = in the workflow mediator, QUEUED = in an event queue, RUNNING = in an active process, COMPLETED = fully completed, RESUBMITTED = resubmitted to the Process Control or Data Queue table, INTERRUPTED = interrupted, ABORTED = stopped by a server shutdown. (Note that RUNNING LRTs are automatically changed to ABORTED LRTs on server startup.)

fields.name: IS_RESUBMITTABLE
fields.description: if Y, the LRT can be resubmitted to its original Process Control or Data Queue. (E.g. Bulk Plan before shipment modifications)

fields.name: IS_INTERRUPTIBLE
fields.description: if Y, the LRT supports clean interruption (e.g. Bulkplan Termination)

fields.name: APP_MACHINE_GID
fields.description: The application server running the LRT.

fields.name: LOG_PROCESS_ID
fields.description: The log process ID of the LRT. This may not be unique across server runs, but corresponds to the Logging ID of the LRT during its runtime.

fields.name: BUSINESS_PROCESS_PID

fields.name: MODE_PROFILE_NAME
fields.description: A user defined name for the mode profile.

fields.name: IS_COMPATIBLE
fields.description: A true or false flag to identify whether the mode profile is compatible???

fields.name: MODE_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the mode profile group.

fields.name: TRANSPORT_MODE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the transport mode within the system. This field defines what transport modes (eg. Air, truck, Barge etc.) are available with the mode profile group.

tables.description: A Monitor_Agent has state reflecting the events of interest of the Monitor_Agent's Agent specified. A Monitor_Agent is an abstraction of several types of specialized agents. They are Planned, Unplanned, Exception, Execution, and Assignment. These are specified by the Montor_Agent_Type_GID reference to the Monitor_Agent_Type table. A Monitor_Agent belongs to a Monitor Profile and therefore can't be "shared" among Monitor Profiles. I.e. each Monitor_Agent_GID is unique. Note: a Monitor Agent belonging to a Monitor Profile Template is referred to as a Monitor Agent Template. Similar to a Monitor Profile "instance" is created from a Monitor Profile Template, a Monitor Agent "instance" is created from a Monitor Agent Template.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: The unique identifier for a monitor agent. If this is a monitor agent belonging to a monitor profile template, then this is the concatentation fo the monitor_agent_xid, '-', and the domain_name. If this monitor agent belongs to a monitor profile, then the xid is generated and guaranteed to be unique.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_XID
fields.description: The external identifier for the monitor agent.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_NAME
fields.description: Text, as entered by the user, for familiar reference.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_TEMPLATE_GID
fields.description: Null if this is a monitor agent template -or- The GID for the monitor agent template from which this monitor agent instance was created.

fields.name: MONITOR_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: The monitor profile to which this monitor agent belongs.

fields.name: AGENT_GID
fields.description: Specifies the agent used to listen for events and perform actions.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_TYPE_GID
fields.description: The unique identifier (foreign key) into the Monitor_Agent_Type table. Types of Agents are MonitorPlannedAgent, MonitorUnplannedAgent, MonitorExceptionAgent, MonitorExecutionAgent, and MonitorAssignmentAgent

fields.name: IS_PERFORM_AGENT_ACTION
fields.description: 'Y' if the monitor agent is to perform its agent's actions after receiving an event of interest.

fields.name: OCCURRENCE_COUNT
fields.description: The number of events received by this agent.

fields.name: EXPIRATION_DATE
fields.description: Used for a MonitorPlannedAgent only. Optionally for a "Template" type, Has the expiration date and time specified if, during the definition, the user wants to specify a specific date and time for this Agent to evaluate to true. For example, 12/31/01 11:59:59 OR Has the expiration time specified (no date) if, during definition, the user wants to specify a time, where the date gets determined during execution. For example, 5:00pm and when the assignment takes place, the instance will specify the date, for example, 9/2/02 5:00pm. OR NULL if the expiration date and time will be determined by the dependency service during execution. . Optionally for an "instance" type, Has the expiration date and time specified if, during the definition, the user specified a specific date and time for this Agent to evaluate to true. For example, 12/31/01 11:59:59 OR Has the expiration time specified with the calculated date from execution. For example, 5:00pm and when the assignment takes place, the instance will specify the date, for example, 9/2/02 5:00pm. OR Date and Time determined by the dependency service during execution

fields.name: LAST_EVENT_OCCUR_DATE
fields.description: Null for a "Template" type OR The date and time the event occurred for the event most recently received by this agent which the query conditions evaluated to true and has its IS_RECORD_EVENT = true.

fields.name: LAST_EVENT_RECEIVED_DATE
fields.description: Null for a "Template" type OR The date and time the event was received for the event most recently received by this agent which the query conditions evaluated to true and has its IS_RECORD_EVENT = true.

fields.name: TIME_RESET_OFFSET
fields.description: For a MonitorPlannedAgent only. The duration, in hours, to which to offset an expiration date. For example, if this agent receives a time event of interest, after performing its action(s), it resets its expiration date to it expiration date + the Time_Reset_Offset and subscribes a workflow trigger to listen for the specified expiration date as an event.

fields.name: TIME_RESET_OFFSET_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for TIME_RESET_OFFSET column.

fields.name: TIME_RESET_OFFSET_BASE
fields.description: TIME_RESET_OFFSET in base units.

fields.name: CONTEXT_OFFSET_BASE
fields.description: CONTEXT_OFFSET in base units.

fields.name: IS_MANDATORY
fields.description: 'Y' indicates that an event of interest to this agent must be received at least once during its "life". Its life is determined to be "over" when the "final" agent has received an event. -OR 'N' indicates that it is required that the Agent does not receive an event during its "life".

fields.name: IS_INIT_EXPIRATION_DATE
fields.description: When a Monitor Profile is initially assigned to a Business Object, subscribe a workflow trigger to listen for the specified expiration date as an event. Note this is for a MonitorPlannedAgent only. 'Y' indicates that when this MonitorTimerAgent is assigned, it uses the event's date and time as the base to initially calculate its expiration date. For example, if this agent is assigned by an event whose date/time occurred 9/01/02 9:00am, and this agents Time_Reset_Offset is 2hrs, then the expiration date is set for 9/01/02 11:00am when assigned to a Business Object.

fields.name: IS_FIRST_MONITORED_EVENT
fields.description: 'Y' if this agent is the first Monitor Planned, and 'N' if this agent is not the first Monitor Planned Agent.

fields.name: IS_LAST_MONITORED_EVENT
fields.description: 'Y' if this agent is the agent in which when an event is received, it is considered to be the "last" event to be received by its Monitor Profile. When this occurs, its "life" is considered over.

fields.name: MAX_OCCURRENCE_COUNT
fields.description: The maximum number of times that the event should be recorded. If the number of occurrences exceeds this number, an exception will be raised. If no there is no desired maximum, then this field should be null.

fields.name: PROCESS_CONTROL_REQUEST_ID
fields.description: If this monitor agent is a monitor planned agent type, and it has a Time Expiration set, the Process Control Request ID is the identifier of the MonitorShipmentTimeBasedEvent published for this Monitor Planned Agent.

fields.name: MONITORED_OBJECT
fields.description: Gid of the object that is being monitored

fields.name: LAST_STATUS_CODE_GID
fields.description: Status code of the last event monitored by this agent

fields.name: LAST_REASON_CODE_GID
fields.description: Reason code of the last event monitored by this agent

fields.name: CONTEXT_SAVED_QUERY_GID
fields.description: The saved query gid when the based on offset = SQLQuery

fields.name: TOLERANCE_RULE_GID
fields.description: Tolerance rule for a planned milestone.

fields.name: IS_RELATED_PROFILING
fields.description: If "Y" , the event received on planned milestone will trigger related profiling.

fields.name: REPLICATION_TYPE
fields.description: The type that the monitor agent is replicate for, such as Equipment, Stop.

fields.name: REPLICATION_PARAMETER
fields.description: The detail information of the replication type. e.g. For Replication_Type='STOP', Replication_Parameter is '-stop_number 1 -location_gid PHL'

tables.description: The MONITOR_AGENT_EVENT is a link for each agent to the list of events which were of interest to the agent and evaluated its saves queries to true.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_EVENT_SEQNO
fields.description: The primary key for uniqueness.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: A foreign key reference to the Monitor_Agent to which the event was of interest and evaluated its saved queries to true.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: The foreign key reference into the ie_shipment_status table for events which are xml based.

fields.name: EVENT_GROUP_GID
fields.description: The foreign key reference into the agent_event_group table.

fields.name: EVENT_OCCUR_DATE
fields.description: The date and time the event occurred.

fields.name: EVENT_RECEIVED_DATE
fields.description: The date and time the event was received.

fields.name: EXCEPTION_MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: The milestone that got an error because of the profiling exception milestone.

fields.name: TRIG_EXPT_MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: The milestone which caused the profiling exception determined by the exception milestone

fields.name: EXCEPTION_AGENT_EVENT_GID
fields.description: This is the GID of the exception event that was detected. (e.g. MONITORED SHIPMENT - MISSING This is the GID of the exception event that was detected. (e.g. MONITORED SHIPMENT - MISSING SEQUENCE MILESTONE)

fields.name: IS_CURRENT_EXCEPTION
fields.description: Indicates that the agent event in case of an exception event is currently valid. This value is reset to 'N' every time an agent that produced the exception event is profiled. If the exception still persists another event record is inserted with a value of 'Y'.

fields.name: PREDECESSOR_MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: GID of the predecessor milestone used to determine this exception.

fields.name: SUCCESSOR_MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: GID of the successor milestone used to determine this exception.

fields.name: STATUS_CODE_GID
fields.description: Status code for this event

fields.name: REASON_CODE_GID
fields.description: Reason code for this event

tables.description: This is the link for a specific dependency service between two Monitor Planned Agents.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_GID
fields.description: Part of the composite primary key. It is a foreign key reference back to the Monitor Agnet 'predecessor' in this link.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_SUCC_GID
fields.description: Part of the composite primary key. It is a foreign key reference back to the monitor agent 'successor' in this link.

fields.name: DEPENDENCY_SERVICE_GID
fields.description: Part of the composite primary key. It is a foreign key reference back to the dependency_service table indicating which type of dependency service this is for this link.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_LINK_SEQ
fields.description: If more than one link for each Monitor_Agent, Monitor_Agent_Successor, this specifies the process order for the Monitor Profile to process these dependency service links. For example, if deactivate and a Time_Link links are between the same Monitor_Agent, Monitor_Agent_Successor, it result will be different if deactivate is processed first or second.

fields.name: DURATION_OFFSET
fields.description: Only for Time_Link, and is optional. If set, it will add the duration_offset, which is specified in hours, to the Monitor_Agent_Successor's expiration Date.

fields.name: EXPIRATION_DATE
fields.description: Only for a Time_Link, and is optional, For a template, it has the expiration date and time specified if, during the definition, the user wants to specify a specific date and time for the Monitor Agent Successor to be set to evaluate to true. For example, 12/31/01 11:59:59 -or- It has the expiration time specified (no date) if, during definition, the user wants to specify a time, where the date gets determined during execution. For example, 5:00pm and when the assignment takes place, the instance will specify the date, for example, 9/2/02 5:00pm. -OR NULL if the expiration date and time will NOT be determined by the dependency service during execution but by the MonitorTimeAgent.

fields.name: DURATION_OFFSET_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for DURATION_OFFSET column.

fields.name: DURATION_OFFSET_BASE
fields.description: DURATION_OFFSET in base units.

tables.description: This table describes the different monitor Agent types which can be one of the five Assignment, Execution, Planned, Unplanned and Exception. There can be only one Assignment and Execution Agent for a single Profile.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_TYPE_GID

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_TYPE_XID
fields.description: The external identifier for the monitor agent type.

fields.name: MONITOR_AGENT_TYPE_NAME
fields.description: The description of the agent type.

tables.description: This table describes all the Businees Fuctions that are available for selection when the dependency service on a link is of the type "Business Function". For example Tender Withdrawal Time

This table defines all the Operands used in the Monitor Tolerance Rule when the rule type is "comparison". For example Planned Arrival. They are also available on the Time details of a Planned Milestone to define the "Based On"
This table definesl the Details of Operands defined in the Monitor Operand table
This table defines all the Operands used in the Monitor Tolerance Rule when the rule type is "comparison". For example "Between"

A monitor profile can be either a Template (Monitor Profile Template) or an 'instance' (Monitor Profile) created from a template. If it is a Template, it will not have an associated business object. If it is an 'instance', it will have an associated business object.

fields.name: MONITOR_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: The unique identier of the monitor profile.

fields.name: MONITOR_PROFILE_XID
fields.description: The external identifier for the monitor profile. It is used to be part of the key for a monitor profile template or monitor profile.

fields.name: MONITOR_PROFILE_NAME
fields.description: Text, as entered by the user, for familiar reference.

fields.name: MONITOR_PROFILE_TEMPLATE_GID
fields.description: Null if this is a monitor profile template -or- the global identifier indicating the monitor profile template from which this monitor profile was created.

fields.name: IS_TEMPLATE
fields.description: True if this is a monitor profile template (default) -or- False if this is a monitor profile.

fields.name: DATA_QUERY_TYPE_GID
fields.description: The unique identifier referring to the data_query_type table to indicate which type of business object (order_base, shipment, order_release, invoice) the monitor_profile is associated. Note: a Monitor Profile must have a value in this column depending on which value this has, the corresponding column (order_base_gid, shipment_gid, order_release_gid, invoice_gid) must have a value. Thus, the type limits the type of agents you can use and events you can listen for. For example, only Shipment events for a shipment data query type.

fields.name: MONITOR_PROFILE_TEMPLATE_GID

fields.name: NFRC_RULE_GID

fields.name: EFFECTIVE_DATE

fields.name: EXPIRATION_DATE
fields.description: Compared against the start time of the primary leg shipment in the job. Used for all charge types.

fields.name: JOB_INVOLVED_PARTY_QUAL_GID

fields.name: JOB_LOCATION_PROFILE_GID

fields.name: OR_ORIGIN_REGION_GID

fields.name: OR_DESTINATION_REGION_GID

fields.name: OR_ORDER_RELEASE_TYPE_GID

fields.name: OR_MIN_TOTAL_WEIGHT

fields.name: OR_MAX_TOTAL_WEIGHT

fields.name: OR_MIN_TOTAL_VOLUME
fields.description: Compared against the summed total volume of all orders in a job. Used for O charge type.

fields.name: OR_MAX_TOTAL_VOLUME
fields.description: Compared against the summed total volume of all orders in a job. Used for O charge type.

fields.name: OR_TRANS_HANDLING_UNIT_GID
fields.description: Compared against the transport handling units (ship unit specs) for all orders in a job. Used for O charge type.

fields.name: BUY_LOCATION_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: Compared against all buy shipment stop locations for all shipments in a job. Used for B charge type.

fields.name: BUY_MODE_PROFILE_GID

fields.name: BUY_PORT_OF_LOAD_LOC_PROF_GID

fields.name: BUY_PORT_OF_DSCH_LOC_PROF_GID

tables.description: Contains the valid list of entries of notification content types. In other words, it a unique identifier for each group of contents that might be in the email (or fax, etc.). For example, a function might be "SHIPMENT_STATUS". A shipment status" email contents would include the shipment_gid, shipment status, date of shipment, etc. These contents that relate to the function is stored in the LOCATION_COM_MAPPING table. A function is then associated with a specific event (like "SHIPMENT STATUS - DELAYED" or "SHIPMENT STATUS - COMPLETED") within the NOTIFY_SUBJECT table. People can then subscribe to an event that they are interested in, and then receive an email notification (or another communication method) when that event occurs.

tables.description: List of valid entries for workflow events that a person might want to subscribe to. Each subject (event) is mapped to a notify_function_gid, which ultimately identifies the email (fax, etc.) contents to send when that event occurs. For example, an event (notify_subject_gid) might include "SHIPMENT STATUS - DELAYED" or "SHIPMENT STATUS - COMPLETED". Each of these subjects are associated with the notify_function_gid of "SHIPMENT_STATUS". A person interested in knowing about these events would subscribe to the subject, and receive in an email (fax, etc.) that might include the shipment_gid, shipment status, date of shipment, etc. (content fields identified through out the notify_function_gid).

tables.description: This table consists of the individual line items for the order base. For e.g., if the order base has an order of: 500 Nike Shoes, from Philly to N.Y. latest by Jan/1/2001 the line items may contain: 200 Nike Shoes, from Philly to N.Y. 200 Socks, from Nashville to N.Y. Etc. Generally the line item should have the same destination for an order base, however that is not a rule. The tags are used in the line item for tracking purposes. These can be any combination of defined serial numbers. Four tags can be specified for tracking at various levels.

tables.description: This is the base table that defines the orders. An order base can have multiple origins, destinations, different times etc. Multiple order releases can be created against one order base. In the XML side, the TransOrder(Transportation Order) is similar to Order base.

fields.description: PAYMENT_METHOD_CODE_GID contains the unique identifier for the payment method code for the order. Examples of payment method include electronic fund transfer and cash on delivery.

fields.name: TIME_WINDOW_EMPHASIS_GID

fields.name: SERVPROV_PROFILE_GID

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: This table maps Oracle codes to a translation id, in order to provide better information to the user on how to fix a problem when an Oracle error arises.

fields.description: A constraint name, such as the foreign key name, if applicable.

tables.description: Order movement represent part of the routing of an order. It is also a collection of ship units that are unplanned for certain part of a route.

fields.description: the unique identifier of an order movement

fields.name: IS_TEMPORARY
fields.description: indicated whether this order movement can coexist with shipments. If this flag is true, the order movement will be removed once the order movement is planned on shipments. Otherwise the order movement's status is set to PLANNED-FINAL and stays in the system.

fields.name: PERSPECTIVE
fields.description: indicate whether this is BUY or SELL

fields.description: the source of the order movement. It can be null

fields.name: IS_FIXED_SRC
fields.description: indicate whether the source location on this order movement is the real source location or it is derived from the source location of its upstream shipments.

fields.name: DEST_LOCATION_GID

fields.name: IS_FIXED_DEST

tables.description: It is the detail table of an order movement. It contains all the s ship units in an order movement.
It represents the fact that a permanent order movement can be partially left unplanned on some temporary order movements
Order movement reference number

This table is linked to the OB_ORDER_BASE table. It specifies the release information against the base order information. An order release can have only single origin and single destination. All line items for the order release information must have the same time windows.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier created by the system for the order release. the gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_XID
fields.description: Same as gid except for the domain portion.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_NAME
fields.description: A name given to the order release by the user.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_TYPE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the type of the order release. For example: Customer order Distribution order Manufacturing order Purchase order etc.

fields.name: ORDER_BASE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the order base in the system. It is system generated. The gid contains the domian portion.

fields.name: SOURCE_LOCATION_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: DEST_LOCATION_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: FIXED_ITINERARY_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: ASSIGNED_ITINERARY_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: FIXED_SELL_ITINERARY_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: SHIP_UNIT_ORIGIN
fields.description: Indicates how the ship units were created (BL=order base by line, BS=order base by ship unit, RL=order release by line, RS=order release by ship unit). If this field is not 'RS' (formerly 'U'), GC3 deletes all existing order ship unit for the given order release, before calling new releasing logic to generate ship unit.

fields.name: TOTAL_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: TOTAL_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: TOTAL_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: TOTAL_VOLUME in base units.

fields.name: TOTAL_ITEM_PACKAGE_COUNT
fields.description: Summary of ITEM_PACKAGE_COUNT of all line items.

fields.name: MAX_LENGTH_BASE
fields.description: MAX_LENGTH in base units.

fields.name: MAX_WIDTH_BASE
fields.description: MAX_WIDTH in base units.

fields.name: MAX_HEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: MAX_HEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: PLAN_FROM_LOCATION_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: PLAN_TO_LOCATION_GID
fields.description: Must be Y or N. Y indicates that the order should not be bundled with any others. Order releasing logic will copy this value from the order base when the order is released.

fields.name: BULK_PLAN_GID
fields.description: If the order was planned via the bulk plan process, then the global identifier for the bulk plan.

fields.name: SELL_BULK_PLAN_GID
fields.description: The bulk plan gid for a sell side bulk plan for this order.

fields.name: TOTAL_SHIP_UNIT_COUNT
fields.description: Number of ship units on this order.

fields.name: LOAD_POINT
fields.description: source load point

fields.name: UNLOAD_POINT
fields.description: dest unload point

fields.name: PICKUP_IS_APPT
fields.description: Indicates if the early/late pickup times are appointment times

fields.name: DELIVERY_IS_APPT
fields.description: Indicates if the early/late delivery times are appointment times

fields.name: PLAN_FROM_LOAD_POINT
fields.description: load point associated with plan from location

fields.name: PLAN_TO_UNLOAD_POINT
fields.description: unload point associated with plan to location

fields.name: PRIM_LEG_SRC_LOC_OVERRIDE_GID
fields.description: Location override ID of source for primary leg shipment

fields.description: Location override ID of the Port of Load for primary leg carrier

fields.name: TEMPLATE_TYPE
fields.description: Hide template created for customer from other order UI. "Customer" is for customer template.

fields.name: BUNDLING_TYPE
fields.description: AUTOMATIC or MANUAL ONLY allow bundling; they are distinguished only by user defined queries. DO NOT BUNDLE means order will never be bundled with other orders.

fields.name: IS_CONSOLIDATE_OR_EQUIPMENT
fields.description: Whether to consolidate order release equipment when bundling.

fields.name: PARTITION_GID
fields.description: acording to this name, bulk plan partition orders to be planned together in one partition

fields.name: TOTAL_DECLARED_VALUE
fields.description: Sum of declared value on ORDER_RELEASE_LINE.

fields.name: BUY_ITINERARY_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: A constraint. Only itineraries compatible with this profile shall be considered when building buy shipments

fields.name: SELL_ITINERARY_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: A constraint. Only itineraries compatible with this profile shall be considered when building sell shipments

fields.name: SELL_SERVPROV_GID
fields.description: indicates the service provider to be used when planning this order onto a sell side shipment

fields.name: SELL_SERVPROV_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: indicates the service provider profile to be used when planning this order onto a sell side shipment

fields.name: IS_IGNORE_LOCATION_CALENDAR
fields.description: if it is Y, location calendar will be ignored when plan this order on to shipment. If NULL, treat it as N

fields.name: RATE_SERVICE_GID
fields.description: Rate service constraint for the order

fields.name: TRANSPORT_HANDLING_UNIT_GID

fields.name: ON_RT_EXECUTION
fields.description: If 'Y': indicates that a shipment built from this order release has been assigned to a route instance leg. For migration: old existing order releases migrated to the default value ('N').

fields.name: IS_SPLITTABLE
fields.description: Indicates whether the Order Release can be split when planning during container optimization.

fields.name: LATEST_EST_DELIVERY_DATE
fields.description: Latest Estimate Delivery Date on the last leg shipments

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: This column defines the priority associated with an Order Release

fields.name: LATEST_EST_PICKUP_DATE
fields.description: Latest Estimated Pickup Date on the first leg shipments.

fields.name: MARKED_FOR_PURGE
fields.description: A boolean field. When it is set to "Y", this row and its child data will be purged.

fields.name: EQUIPMENT_TYPE_GID
fields.description: Equipment Type Constaints for Equipment Assignment.

fields.name: EM_PHONE_NUMBER
fields.description: Emergency phone number when shipping Hazardous Materials. The 24 hours contact for the shipment.

fields.name: IS_PRE_ENTERED_PU
fields.description: When this field is check, indicate if this order is a pre-entered pickup

fields.name: EARLIEST_EST_PICKUP_DATE
fields.description: Earliest Estimated Pickup Date on the first leg shipments.

tables.description: This table consists the links to the Accessorial table to define the accessories required to fulfill the order at the order release level. It supercedes the accessories defined at the order base level. For e.g., a pump may be required to pump out the chemicals from a delivery truck. Accessorials are different from special services in that the customers are charged for the accessories.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier created by the system for the order release. the gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

tables.description: Stores allocated freight costs for accruals.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_ACCRUAL_GID
fields.description: Unique Id for this accrual entry

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_ACCRUAL_XID
fields.description: Unique Id for this accrual entry

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_GID
fields.description: Order release id for which this accrual is related

fields.name: SENT_DATE
fields.description: Timestamp of when this accrual was sent from GC3, null means it has not been sent yet.

fields.name: FREIGHT_COST
fields.description: Total current allocated freight cost

fields.name: FREIGHT_SENT_COST
fields.description: Total allocated freight cost already sent via the accrual interface.

fields.name: IS_REVERSAL
fields.description: A flag to determine whether the Order Release Accrual is a reversal.

fields.name: SHIPMENT_START_DATE
fields.description: The Shipment Start Date.

fields.name: FREIGHT_COST_FN
fields.description: functional currency amount for freight_cost

fields.name: FREIGHT_SENT_C_FN
fields.description: functional currency amount for freight_sent_cost

fields.name: DELTA
fields.description: delta or difference for the accrual calculated as: new amount - amount previously sent

fields.name: DELTA_FN
fields.description: functional currency amount for delta

fields.name: COST_TYPE_GID
fields.description: if line accruals are enabled, this will be populated with the cost type for the accrual line

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID
fields.description: if line accruals are enabled, this will be populated with the accessorial id for the accrual line

tables.description: The many to many join table for order release and Invoices/Bills.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier created by the system for the order release. the gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: BILL_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: DOMAIN_NAME
fields.description: Domain of the User. Is used for creating the GID.

tables.description: Order release involved parties.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_GID
fields.description: id for the order release the involved party is linked to

fields.name: INVOLVED_PARTY_QUAL_GID
fields.description: qualifier id for the involved party

fields.name: INVOLVED_PARTY_CONTACT_GID
fields.description: contact id for the involved party

fields.name: COM_METHOD_GID
fields.description: communication method id for the involved party

tables.description: ORDER_RELEASE_LINE contains the individual line items for an order release. (See also OB_LINE.) For example, if the order base contains: 500 Nike shoes from Philadelphia to N.Y. at the latest by Jan. 1, 2001, the order release may contain a release of 400 Nike shoes by Dec. 1, 2000 and the line item for the order release may contain 400 Nike shoes from Philadelphia to N.Y. and 400 socks from Nashville to N.Y.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_LINE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the multiple order release lines per order release. The gid has the domain portion.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_LINE_XID
fields.description: Similar to the gid except for the domain.

fields.name: ORDER_RELEASE_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier created by the system for the order release. the gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: WEIGHT
fields.description: The weight of the item.

fields.name: WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for WEIGHT column.

fields.name: WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: VOLUME
fields.description: Th volume of the item if applicable.

fields.name: VOLUME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for VOLUME column.

fields.name: VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: VOLUME in base units.

fields.name: TAG1
fields.description: Additional information about the item. Like the SPLC code.

fields.name: TAG2
fields.description: Additional information about the item. Like the SPLC code.

fields.name: TAG3
fields.description: Additional information about the item. Like the SPLC code.

fields.name: TAG4
fields.description: Additional information about the item. Like the SPLC code.

fields.name: DECLARED_VALUE
fields.description: The declared cost of the item.

fields.name: DECLARED_VALUE_BASE
fields.description: DECLARED_VALUE in base units.

fields.name: DECLARED_VALUE_CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: PACKAGED_ITEM_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: BUY_GENERAL_LEDGER_GID
fields.description: General Ledger code for order release line.

fields.name: HAZ_SUBSIDIARY_HAZARD
fields.description: Hazmat subrisk class

fields.name: PRICE_PER_UNIT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: The Unit of Measurement this Unit Price is based on.

fields.name: PACKAGE_LENGTH
fields.description: package length for order release line; overrides length on packaged item

fields.name: PACKAGE_WIDTH
fields.description: package width for order release line; overrides width on packaged item

fields.name: PACKAGE_HEIGHT
fields.description: package height for order release line; overrides height on packaged item

fields.name: PACKAGE_DIAMETER
fields.description: package diameter for order release line; overrides diameter on packaged item

fields.name: PACKAGE_CORE_DIAMETER
fields.description: package core diameter for order release line; overrides core diameter on packaged item

fields.name: ORDER_BASE_GID
fields.description: The Order Base from which this Order Release Line was built.

fields.name: OB_LINE_GID
fields.description: The Order Base Line from which this Order Release Line was built.

fields.name: HAZ_ALL_PACKED
fields.description: Flag to indicate the pkg is 'All packed in one'

fields.name: HAZ_AUTHORIZATION
fields.description: The authorization for the Dangerous Goods shipment.The authorization field is only required to state special governmental authorizations that are required because of the provisions or packing materials shipped with.

fields.name: HAZ_CHEMICAL_FORMULA
fields.description: The basic chemical description of the substance

fields.name: HAZ_CONCENTRATION_PERCENT
fields.description: Required if the Dangerous Goods UN Number is 2031, 1796, 1826, or 1873. Enter the percentage of DG concentration if the above condition applies.

fields.name: HAZ_CRIT_SAFETY_INDEX
fields.description: A measurement for packages containing fissile material that is determined by dividing the number 50 by the smaller of the numbers determined using the rules in section 10.6.2.4.3 of the DG Regulation Guide

fields.name: HAZ_IDG_ADD_DESC_INFO
fields.description: Hazmat IDG additional description information

fields.name: HAZ_MAX_QUANTITY
fields.description: Maximum passenger volume or the Maximum cargo volume for the item (Used in Q- Value calculation)

fields.name: HAZ_OUTER_PACKAGING_TYPE
fields.description: Outer packaging type if packing type = All packed in one

fields.name: HAZ_OUTER_PACKING_COUNT
fields.description: Outer Packing count

fields.name: HAZ_IS_OVERPACK
fields.description: Flag to indicate the pkg is an 'overpack'

fields.name: HAZ_PACKAGE_TYPE
fields.description: Hazmat Package type of the Dangerous Goods shipment.

fields.name: HAZ_PACKING_COUNT
fields.description: Packing Count (number of packaged units)

fields.name: HAZ_PACKING_INSTRUCTIONS
fields.description: Required for dangerous goods shipments, unless the DG class is radioactive or Not Restricted.See Table 4.2 columns G, I, or K of the IATA DG Regulations.

fields.name: HAZ_PHYSICAL_FORM
fields.description: A description of the physical form of the radioactive substance : S(p)ecial (S)olid (L)iquid (G)as

fields.name: HAZ_Q_VALUE
fields.description: Ratio used to indicate that the combined quantities in 'all packed in one' package are within the maximum allowable limits.

fields.name: HAZ_QUANTITY
fields.description: Hazmat item quantity

fields.name: HAZ_RADIOACTIVE_LABEL
fields.description: The type of label on the shipment. W1 (White I) Y2 (Yellow II) Y3 (Yellow III)

fields.name: HAZ_RAD_PACKAGING
fields.description: A description of the type of packaging the substance is contained in. Acceptable package types are listed in section 10.5 of the DG Regulations Guide: Expected ( Expected ) Industrial ip1 ( Industrial Type 1(IP-1) ) Industrial ip2 ( Industrial Type 2(IP-2) ) Industrial ip3 ( Industrial Type 3(IP-3) ) Type a ( Type A ) Type b(m) ( Type B(M) ) Type b(u) ( Type B(U) ) Type c ( Type C )

fields.name: HAZ_RADIOACTIVITY
fields.description: A measurement of the activity of the shipment. For fissile material, the weight of the material in grams or kilograms may be substituted for the activity measurement

fields.name: HAZ_RADIOACTIVITY_UNITS
fields.description: The units that the activity were mesaured in. This must be in becquerel units (or multiples thereof) : Bq (becquerel) kBq (kilobecquerel) MBq (megabecquerel) GBq (gigabecquerel) TBq (terabecquerel) PBq (petabecquerel)

fields.name: HAZ_RADIONUCLIDE
fields.description: The radionuclide assigned to the substance being shipped. A table of basic radionuclides is available in the DG Regulation Guide section 10.4.A. For example, Aluminium (13) has a radionuclide Al-26

fields.name: HAZ_SPECIAL_PROVISIONS
fields.description: Indicates Dangerous Goods (DG) are being shipped under one of the special provisions listed in the IATA Dangerous Goods Regulation Guide, section 4.4. There appear to be 146 different provisions, and multiple provisions may apply to a single item in some cases.

fields.name: HAZ_SUBSTANCE_NUMBER
fields.description: Hazardous Materials substance number.

fields.name: HAZ_SURFACE_READING
fields.description: The surface reading of the radiation exposure for the package. This reading must be measured in mrem/h

fields.name: HAZ_TRANSPORT_INDEX
fields.description: A number based on the radiation exposure of a package. This is determined based on the maximum measurement of the radiation in mrem/h at a distance of 1 meter from the package surface

fields.name: HAZ_TYPE
fields.description: Hazardous Materials Type. Valid values are: l = Limited Quantities e = 500 KG Exemption f = Fully Regulated (Purolater)

fields.name: HAZ_UNITS
fields.description: IATA units for Non UPS shipments (L=>Liters g=>Grams kg=>Kilograms kg G=>Kilograms Gross).

fields.name: HAZ_EXT_CODE
fields.description: Item Hazmat code as configured in the TNT EM. (For carrier TNT)

fields.name: OB_LINE_REL_INSTR_SEQ
fields.description: Order Base Line Release Instruction Sequence number, carried over from column OB_RELEASE_INSTRUCTION_SEQ in table OB_RELEASE_INSTRUCTION when Order Release Line is created from Order Base Line.

fields.name: IS_SPLIT_ALLOWED
fields.description: Indicates if the order release ship units that are created can be split into multiple pieces of equipment

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified. Possible values are: UPDATE_OK/UPDATE_NOT_OK/MANY.

tables.description: This table and OUT_XMl_PROFILE_D table together specify the outbound xml profiles that can be used to limit the size of xml documents pushed out from gc3 to external systems. The Out_xml_profile table contains the header record for the profile and defines the "gid" for that profile. The out_xml_profile_d table provides the details which comprise a given profile. Each record in the out_xml_profile_d table indicates a specific "chunk" of xml that should be excluded from the outbound document.

fields.name: OUT_XML_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: Globally unique identifier for an outbound xml profile.

fields.name: OUT_XML_PROFILE_XID
fields.description: Domain unique identifier for an outbound xml profile.

fields.name: DEFAULT_MODE
fields.description: Specifies whether the default mode for generating XML should be MIN or MAX. When not specified, the property is the default.

fields.name: SHOULD_VALIDATE
fields.description: Specifies if the XML produced should conform to the schema.

fields.name: XML_TEMPLATE_GID
fields.description: XML template ID

fields.name: USE_TEMPLATE
fields.description: If it equals "Y", integration will use template to build the XML

fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: Contains an entry for each iteration of a workflow run that was kicked off from a process control request. If a process is run only once, then there is one record in the process_control_request table and the PROCESS_CONTROL_HISTORY table. However, if a process is set up to run 5 cycles, then the instructions would show up once in the PROCESS_CONTROL_REQUEST table, but 5 records would exist in the PROCESS_CONTROL_HISTORY table.

fields.name: TOPIC_CLASS_NAME
fields.description: Added for identifying History of a Process Control Request for Report.

fields.name: TOPIC_PARAMETERS
fields.description: Added for identifying History of a Process Request.

tables.description: Contains an entry for each process request. An entry could indicate a one-time kickoff of a workflow, or could be an ongoing job, with a specified interval. For example, a process could be Print Invoices (topic_alias_gid), which is run every day at 6am (next_process_time). If the number_of_cycles equals "-1", then the process will resubmit itself indefinitely for another run. If the TOPIC_ALIAS_GID is null, then GC3 will look at the TOPIC_CLASS_NAME, the java class, to know what workflow to run.

fields.name: PROP_INSTRUCTION_SET_GID
fields.description: Property Set GID

fields.name: PROP_SEQUENCE_NUM
fields.description: Sequence of instruction within the property set.

fields.name: INSTRUCTION
fields.description: Instruction type: SET = set a single or multi-valued property; REMOVE = remove a property value; INCLUDE = include another property set

fields.name: KEY
fields.description: Property key. For INCLUDE, this is the Property Set GID being included.

fields.name: VALUE
fields.description: Property Value. For REMOVE, this can be omitted. It should be set to remove a single value from a multi-valued proeprty. For INCLUDE, this is ignored.

fields.name: DESCRIPTION
fields.description: Description of the property instruction

tables.description: Set of property instructions. The set can be invoked from a property file via the !includeSet directive, or from another set via the Include instruction.

fields.description: Property Set GID

fields.name: PROP_INSTRUCTION_SET_GID
fields.description: Property Set GID

fields.name: LOG_SEQUENCE_NUM
fields.description: Sequence of item within the log.

fields.name: INSTRUCTION
fields.description: Property instruction

fields.name: KEY
fields.description: Property key. For INCLUDE, this is the Property Set GID.

fields.name: VALUE
fields.description: Property value. For REMOVE, this can be set to a particular value of a multi-valued property to remove. Otherwise, it is left blank. For INCLUDE, it is blank.

fields.name: PREVIOUS_VALUE
fields.description: Previous value of property prior to the instruction.

fields.name: PREVIOUS_VALUE_SOURCE
fields.description: Source of property value prior to the instruction. This source is prefixed by: File, if it is from a property file; Set, if it is from a property set.

fields.name: ACTION
fields.description: Action taken to modify the property set: ADD - the instruction was added; CHANGE - the instruction was modified; REMOVE - the instruction was removed.

fields.name: REASON
fields.description: The reason for the property instruction modification.

tables.description: Contains the valid listing of attributes, or rate characteristics. Each attribute is associated with a java class. Each java class will contain one or more fields, which determines the fields the user sees for entering this type of rate information. One or more attributes are then associated with the offering type within the RATE_ATTRIBUTE_JOIN table.

tables.description: Joins together the offering type (such as air) to the attributes required for entering that type of offering. For example, air travel might require a user to enter maximum weight allowed, handling charges, and general contract info. Each of these attributes can be associated with one or more fields. These three attributes would then be associated with the "AIR" offering type, which would then determine the fields that appear to the user for entering rate information.

tables.description: Also known as a lane, a rate geo is a prescribed course for ships, aircraft, etc. that can have costing (or time of travel) associated with it. A rate geo may be something as general as West Coast to East Coast, country to country, state to state, or as specific as zip code to zip code. GC3 will always look at the most granular rate_geo it can find.

fields.name: ROUNDING_TYPE
fields.description: The type of rounding that will occur during rating. Value are: N=no rounding,I=round to nearest interval,F=floor, and C=ceiling.

fields.name: ROUNDING_INTERVAL
fields.description: Numbers will be rounded (or truncated) to the nearest increment of this value.

fields.name: ROUNDING_FIELDS_LEVEL
fields.description: Determines which kinds of fields will be affected by the rounding settings during costing. Values are 0-7, where 0=all fields, 1=none, 2=numeric fields only, 3=unit fields (weight, etc.), 4=currency, 5=numeric and unit fields, 6=numeric and currency fields, and 7=unit and currency fields.

fields.name: ROUNDING_APPLICATION
fields.description: Determines when to apply the rounding rules. Values are A/N/C/O where A=always, N=never, C=conditionals only, and O=cost calculations only.

fields.name: DEFICIT_CALCULATIONS_TYPE
fields.description: When to apply deficit calculations. Values are Y/N/P where Y = Yes, N = No and P = Payant Pour

fields.name: IS_QUOTE

tables.description: Anticipated additional costing due to requirements of the type of shipment for that particular travel path (lane). For example, a carrier may charge extra for handling large loads on trucks into New York City, while another may not. By anticipating these additional costs, the GC3 engine can better determine which carrier is really going to be the most economical for a specific shipment.

fields.name: ROUNDING_TYPE
fields.description: The type of rounding that will occur during rating. Value are: N=no rounding,I=round to nearest interval,F=floor, and C=ceiling.

fields.name: ROUNDING_INTERVAL
fields.description: Numbers will be rounded (or truncated) to the nearest increment of this value.

fields.name: ROUNDING_FIELDS_LEVEL
fields.description: Determines which kinds of fields will be affected by the rounding settings during costing. Values are 0-7, where 0=all fields, 1=none, 2=numeric fields only, 3=unit fields (weight, etc.), 4=currency, 5=numeric and unit fields, 6=numeric and currency fields, and 7=unit and currency fields.

fields.name: ROUNDING_APPLICATION
fields.description: Determines when to apply the rounding rules. Values are A/N/C/O where A=always, N=never, C=conditionals only, and O=cost calculations only.

fields.name: DEFICIT_CALCULATIONS_TYPE
fields.description: When to apply deficit calculations. Values are Y/N/P where Y = Yes, N = No and P = Payant Pour

fields.name: IS_CONTRACT_RATE

fields.name: USE_TACT_AS_DISPLAY_RATE_1
fields.description: If Y, the build shipment logic will invoke the rating engine a second time, restricting it's search to TACT rates only, and apply the result to the shipment as a display rate.

fields.name: IS_DIRECT_ONLY_RATE
fields.description: If Y, this rate will only be applicable to direct shipments.

fields.name: HAZARDOUS_RATE_TYPE
fields.description: N - Applies to shipments with no hazardous items, H - Applies only to shipments with hazardous items, A - Applies to hazardous and non-hazardous shipments

fields.name: USE_TACT_AS_DISPLAY_RATE_2
fields.description: If Y, the build shipment logic will invoke the rating engine a second time, restricting it's search to TACT rates only, and apply the result

fields.name: USE_TACT_AS_DISPLAY_RATE_3

fields.name: MIN_TENDER_LEAD_TIME
fields.description: The lower bound of a tender lead time. That is, if tender lead time of a shipment is less than this lower bound, rate would be invalid.

fields.name: MAX_TENDER_LEAD_TIME
fields.description: The upper bound of tender lead time. That is, if tender lead time of a shipment is bigger than this upper bound, rate would be invalid.

fields.name: PACKAGE_WEIGHT_MIN
fields.description: Not a constraint. Weight value used when calculating total shipment minimum package weight. During rating, it will be multiplied by the number of packages on the shipment, and accessed by the SHIPMENT.MINPKGWEIGHT RBI

fields.name: MAX_AVERAGE_PKG_WEIGHT
fields.description: Constraint. If the total shipment weight/number of packages > this constraint, the rate will not be considered.

fields.name: PACKAGE_COUNT_METHOD
fields.description: Determines how packages are counted when testing the MAX_AVERAGE_PKG_WEIGHT constraint. L - Use line item package count. U - Ship unit count

fields.name: INITIAL_REST_TIME
fields.description: The initial rest time for the rate service before the driver starts to drive. This is actually a buffer added to the simulated run, since otherwise it assumed that a fully rested driver is available for the drive. By adding the extra time, it lessens the chance that an order delivery window won't be met because the driver needed to rest from a previous run before starting the new drive.

tables.description: This table is used to estimate the time of delivery. It stores estimated average speed for ranges of distances, and all includes minimum times. It assumes that the farther the distance, the faster the average speed will be. Ex: A truck on average might drive 10 miles/hr for a distance of 20 miles, because of the amount of time it takes to drive out of the town, get on the higway, and then immediately slow down to deliver the goods to the next town. However, if the truck was taking the goods across 500 miles, then the average speed might be 40 miles an hour, since the truck is on the highway much longer. Based upon a lookup in this table, a rough estimate of delivery can be made.

tables.description: A list of values for unique rate service types. A service type determines how a rate service is calculated. Example of service type: LOOKUP. This means that the rate service is determined by looking up a value in a table. Example 2: DAYDURATION. This means that the delivery is needed within a particular number of business days, and the calculation will include a lookup within a calendar to calculate expected delivery date.

fields.name: DATA_TYPE

fields.name: LOOKUP_TYPE

tables.description: A valid list of rate zone groupings. This is then used within the rate_zone_profile_d table to group lanes together. Example of purpose in which you might want to group lanes: You may want to associate a timeframe of delivery for your lanes, so you would like to create groupings of lanes that share similar durations of time for delivery. If you are associating a delivery timeframe within a day's estimation, then the lane from San Franscisco to Los Angeles = 1 day, and the lane from San Franscisco to Sacramento = 1 day. Rather than entering 1 day for each, these could be put in one profile, and then the profile could state that the timeframe = 1 day. Another lane could be from San Francisco to Philadelphia, which obviously has its own duration, and therefore would not share the same profile. The profile's timeframe would be stored in the table RATE_ZONE_SERVICE_TIME.

If you are associating a delivery timeframe within a day's estimation, then the lane from San Franscisco to Los Angeles = 1 day, and the lane from San Franscisco to Sacramento = 1 day. Rather than entering 1 day for each, these could be put in one profile, and then the profile could state that the timeframe = 1 day. The profile's timeframe would be stored in the table RATE_ZONE_SERVICE_TIME.

tables.description: RG_SPECIAL_SERVICE contains the special services or equipment required for a lane. For example, a lane that goes from California to Alaska might require that the carrier use an airplane that can land in water. The special services entered in this table are required by the shipment but can't necessarily be performed by all carriers. The GC3 engine will check the table before looking at the cost information to determine which carriers can potentially handle the shipment. It does this because the cheapest carrier who doesn't have the special equipment cannot do the job regardless of cost.

fields.description: The RUSHHOUR activity on this calendar defines the rush hour period for this rush hour delay to be applied. For exampl, this calendar may defined MORNING 6-9 as the RUSHHOUR, then shipments match to this x-lane that leave a stop between 6-9 in the morning will have a 2 hour delay if the RUSH_HOUR_DELAY_TIME is 2 hour

fields.description: If this field is true, then if depature time from a stop falls into the calendar rush hour period, apply the rush hour delay time. If this field is false, then apply the rush hour delay when arrival time at the to-stop falls into the rush hour calendar.

tables.description: This table keeps service template info for buyer.

Associates a timeframe with a rate geo. For instance, you could specify that it takes 6 hours to get from San Francisco to Los Angeles. If you would prefer to group lanes together (time estimates don't need to be so specific), then you could create a RATE_ZONE_PROFILE, and then associate lanes together within the RATE_ZONE_PROFILE_D table. The service time would then be associated in the RATE_ZONE_SERVICE_TIME table.

tables.description: Table (and associated tables of SHIPMENT_STOP, SHIPMENT_STOP_D, S_SHIP_UNIT_LINE, and S_SHIP_UNIT) was added in 2.5 to indicate what was actually shipped. This module was added because in 2.1 if the quantity received was different than what was requested, the ORDER_RELEASE information got overwritten. There was no way of comparing how accurate the actual shipments were to what was requested. These tables look very similar to the tables associated with ORDER_RELEASE. The key to this table is SHIPMENT_GID, because there is one record for every actual shipment in this table.
