tables.description: This table stores departure and arrival information about every stop along the way for the life of the shipment (both planned and actual). The key is both the SHIPMENT_GID and the STOP_NUM.

fields.name: SHIPMENT_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: STOP_NUM
fields.description: This field defines the stop number within the shipment. Stop Num = 1 is the Pickup stop, and the max (Stop Num) for a shipment is the delivery stop.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: LOCATION_ROLE_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: PLANNED_ARRIVAL
fields.description: The planned date and time of arrival at the stop.

fields.name: ESTIMATED_ARRIVAL
fields.description: The estimated time of arrival at the stop. It represents the up to the minute ETA that takes into account ETA updates. This date is editable by the carrier or load planner and should incorporate any known delays to delivery. It represents to the best of our knowledge, when we expect the shipment to arrive.

fields.name: ACTUAL_ARRIVAL
fields.description: The actual arrival date and time at the stop.

fields.name: PLANNED_DEPARTURE
fields.description: The planned departure date and time at the stop.

fields.name: ESTIMATED_DEPARTURE
fields.description: The estimated time of departure for the stop. It represents the up to the minute ETA that takes into account ETA updates. This date is editable by the carrier or load planner and should incorporate any known delays to departure. It represents to the best of our knowledge, when we expect the shipment to depart from the stop.

fields.name: ACTUAL_DEPARTURE
fields.description: The actual departure date and time at the stop.

fields.name: WAIT_TIME
fields.description: The time the truck or the transportation mode has to wait at the stop.

fields.name: WAIT_TIME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for WAIT_TIME column.

fields.name: WAIT_TIME_BASE
fields.description: WAIT_TIME in base units.

fields.name: REST_TIME
fields.description: The time the truck or the transportation mode should take for rest at the stop.

fields.name: REST_TIME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for REST_TIME column.

fields.name: REST_TIME_BASE
fields.description: REST_TIME in base units.

fields.name: DRIVE_TIME
fields.description: The drive time to teh stop from the last stop.

fields.name: DRIVE_TIME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for DRIVE_TIME column.

fields.name: DRIVE_TIME_BASE
fields.description: DRIVE_TIME in base units.

fields.name: DIST_FROM_PREV_STOP
fields.description: The distance from the last stop.

fields.name: DIST_FROM_PREV_STOP_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for DIST_FROM_PREV_STOP column.

fields.name: DIST_FROM_PREV_STOP_BASE
fields.description: DIST_FROM_PREV_STOP in base units.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_TIME
fields.description: The time spent at the stop for pickup or delivery.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_TIME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for ACTIVITY_TIME column.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_TIME_BASE
fields.description: ACTIVITY_TIME in base units.

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_TIME
fields.description: The sum of fixed_accessorial_time for a particular stop.

fields.name: PARENT_LOCATION_GID
fields.description: If an operational location, such as terminal at a port, is chosen for this stop, the LOCATION_GID will be copied to this field and the LOCATION_GID field will contain the operational location.

fields.name: STOP_REQUIREMENT_TYPE
fields.description: This field is currently used only for TRIPs.

fields.name: ARBITRARY_TYPE
fields.description: SOURCE or DEST to indicate the type of arbitrary. If null, this stop is not an arbitrary.

fields.name: IS_FIXED_DISTANCE
fields.description: When set to Y, indicates the existence of a user-entered value for the dis_from_prev_stop field

fields.name: VOYAGE_GID
fields.description: Refers column VOYAGE.VOYAGE_GID.

fields.name: IS_MOTHER_VESSEL
fields.description: Desginating a leg as mother vessel to automatically populated header.

fields.name: FLIGHT_INSTANCE_ID
fields.description: Refers column FLIGHT_INSTANCE. FLIGHT_INSTANCE GID.

fields.name: STOP_TYPE
fields.description: Define the type of this stop.

fields.name: DRIVER_NON_PAYABLE
fields.description: Allows the user to specify that stop is payable or not.

fields.name: DISTANCE_TYPE
fields.description: L - Loaded, B- Bobtail, D - Deadhead

fields.name: RATE_DISTANCE_GID
fields.description: This column would give the rate distance to be used when finding distance from this stop to next stop. This is done mainly for arbitrary case.

fields.name: ROUTE_CODE_GID
fields.description: This column would give the route code to be used when finding distance from this stop to next stop This is done mainly for handling arbitrary case.

fields.description: Identifies whether the reference numbers with this qualifier should display on related shipments.Possible Values are Y/N.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified. Possible values are: UPDATE_OK/UPDATE_NOT_OK/MANY.

tables.description: This table stores the unit level information at the order release level. It is a parallel table to S_SHIP_UNIT table (at the Shipment level).

fields.name: IS_SPLITABLE
fields.description: A true or false flag to identify if the ship unit can be split when creating an order.

fields.name: UNIT_LENGTH
fields.description: The length of each ship unit. If this row represents 20 same ship units, total length is 20 x UNIT_LENGTH.

fields.name: UNIT_LENGTH_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_LENGTH column.

fields.name: UNIT_LENGTH_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_LENGTH in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_WIDTH
fields.description: it is the weight of each ship_unit (each ro may represent SHIP_UNIT_COUNT number of ship units). If this field is null, or is 0, the weight of this ship_unit is computed as the total of container weight from its ship_unit_spec and the weight of its ship_unit_lines.

fields.name: UNIT_WIDTH_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_WIDTH column.

fields.name: UNIT_WIDTH_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_WIDTH in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_HEIGHT
fields.description: The height of each ship unit. If this row represents 20 same ship units, total height is 20 x UNIT_HEIGHT.

fields.name: UNIT_HEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_HEIGHT column.

fields.name: UNIT_HEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_HEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_WEIGHT
fields.description: Weight of the shipping unit.

fields.name: UNIT_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_WEIGHT column.

fields.name: UNIT_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_VOLUME
fields.description: it is the volume of this ship_unit (each ro may represent SHIP_UNIT_COUNT number of ship units). If this field is null, or is 0, the volume of this ship_unit is computed as the total of container volume from its ship_unit_spec and the volume of its ship_unit_.

fields.name: UNIT_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_VOLUME in base units.

fields.description: the number of packages in this SHIP_UNIT_LINE for one ship unit ( its S_SHIP_UNIT may represent more than one same ship units). (e.g., for 20 ship units that contains 4 cases of 24 cans of coca, each S_SHIP_UNIT_LINE row will have ITEM_GID: coca, ITEM_COUNT 4x24, ITEM_PACKAGE_SPEC_GID: case-of-24-cans, ITEM_PACKAGE_COUNT: 4. In the SHIP_UNIT table, the SHIP_UNIT_COUNT will be 20)

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified. Possible values are: UPDATE_OK/UPDATE_NOT_OK/MANY.

tables.description: This is the master table that holds various special services required for transportation, pickup and delivery. A special service differs from an accessory in that the customer does not have to pay for using the special service. An example of a special service can be a pump required to pull the chemicals from a delivery truck. The special services defined can be linked at the item level or at the location level in GC3. The logic in GC3 checks to see if a special service is required and thus, schedules the pick-up or delivery accordingly.

fields.name: SPECIAL_SERVICE_GID
fields.description: SPECIAL_SERVICE_GID contains the unique identifier for the special service for the item being shipped.

fields.name: SPECIAL_SERVICE_XID
fields.description: SPECIAL_SERVICE_XID contains the unique exterbak identifier for the special service for the item being shipped.

fields.name: PAYABLE_INDICATOR_GID
fields.description: indicates the default payable status of this special service

fields.name: BILLABLE_INDICATOR_GID
fields.description: indicates the default billable status of this special service

fields.name: SPECIAL_SERVICE_DESC
fields.description: SPECIAL_SERVICE_DESC contains the description for the special service for the item being shipped.

fields.name: MODE_PROFILE_GID
fields.description: MODE_PROFILE_GID contains the unique identifier for the mode profile for the item being shipped.

fields.name: IS_ARBITRARY
fields.description: Designates whether or not a special service is an arbitrary type special service.

fields.name: SPECL_SRV_GROUP_GID
fields.description: Allow user to organize special services by group

fields.description: Designates this special service is to be checked during driver assignment.

fields.description: Designates this special service is to be checked during power unit assignment.

fields.description: STCC_GID contains the unique identifier for the Standard Commodity Code (STCC) for the item being shipped.

fields.name: STCC_XID
fields.description: STCC_XID contains the unique external identifier for the Standard Commodity Code (STCC) for the item being shipped.

fields.name: STCC_NAME
fields.description: STCC_NAME contains the Standard Commodity Code (STCC) name of the item being shipped.

fields.name: IS_49_CODE

fields.name: ARG_SOURCE

tables.description: Used to specify the fields on the message table that should be set from the message format.

fields.description: Number identifying the equipment used, which is not represented by a record in GC3's EQUIPMENT table (use EQUIPMENT_GID for that).

fields.name: EQUIPMENT_NUMBER
fields.description: Number identifying the equipment used, which is not represented by a record in GC3'S EQUIPMENT table (use EQUIPMENT_GID for that).

fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: EQUIPMENT_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: WEIGHT_QUALIFIER
fields.description: Indicates what the SCALE_WEIGHT and TARE_WEIGHT fields represent: Estimate, Net or Gross weight,

fields.name: SCALE_WEIGHT
fields.description: Weight recorded by scale when equipment is loaded.

fields.name: SCALE_WEIGHT_UOM
fields.description: Weight UOM for SCALE_WEIGHT

fields.name: SCALE_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: Weight base.

fields.name: TARE_WEIGHT

fields.name: TARE_WEIGHT_BASE

fields.name: SCALE_TICKET

fields.name: INTERMODAL_EQUIP_LENGTH
fields.description: Specially formatted field that is required by the railroads, representing the length of the equipement. It is in the format FtFtInIn, so 4803 would represent 48 feet, 3 inches.

fields.name: SUBSTITUTE_EQUIPMENT_GROUP_GID
fields.description: Equipment group substituted by carrier for this equipments

fields.name: TYPE_ASSIGNMENT_CODE

fields.name: ASSIGNMENT_CODE
fields.description: Indicates the source of the EQUIPMENT_GID

fields.name: PICK_SKU_GID
fields.description: With PICK_SKU_QUANTITY_TYPE_GID, this identifies the pickup inventory bucket adjusted during equipment type assignment.

fields.name: DROP_SKU_GID
fields.description: With DROP_SKU_QUANTITY_TYPE_GID, this identifies the dropoff inventory bucket adjusted during equipment type assignment.

fields.name: FLOOR_HEIGHT
fields.description: Actual height of the platform or floor above the ground or the rail.

fields.name: EMPTY_CG_HEIGHT
fields.description: When empty, indicates the height from Top of Rail to the Center of Gravity

fields.name: DRY_CAPACITY
fields.description: The maximum interior capacity of the equipment

fields.name: LIQUID_CAPACITY
fields.description: The number of gallons the equipment will hold

fields.name: CALCULATED_GROSS_WT
fields.description: The actual sum of the cargo plus the actual tare weight. A calculated version of a scale weight.

fields.name: ACTUAL_LOAD_LIMIT
fields.description: The maximum permissible weight of the commodity that can be loaded into the equipment.

fields.name: ACTUAL_LENGTH
fields.description: The inside length of the actual equipment from end to end inside walls, linings, and permanent bulkheads

fields.name: ACTUAL_WIDTH
fields.description: The inside width of the actual equipment from side walls and linings

fields.name: ACTUAL_HEIGHT
fields.description: The inside height of the actual equipment from the floor to the lowest point of the interior ceiling

tables.description: This table stores the shipment ship unit information. This table was added to identify the actual shipment ship unit as opposed to order release ship unit (in earlier versions of GC3).

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_GID
fields.description: A unique pointer to define the shipment ship unit. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_XID
fields.description: Similar to the gid except for the domain portion.

fields.name: UNIT_LENGTH
fields.description: The length of each s_ship unit. If this row represents 20 same ship units, total length is 20 x UNIT_LENGTH.

fields.name: UNIT_LENGTH_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_LENGTH column.

fields.name: UNIT_LENGTH_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_LENGTH in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_WIDTH
fields.description: : it is the weight of each s_ship_unit (each row may represent SHIP_UNIT_COUNT number of same ship units). If this field is null, or is 0, the weight of this s_ship_unit is computed as the total of container weight from its ship_unit_spec and the weight of its s_ship_unit_lines mutilply.

fields.name: UNIT_WIDTH_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_WIDTH column.

fields.name: UNIT_WIDTH_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_WIDTH in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_HEIGHT
fields.description: The height of each s_ship unit. If this row represents 20 same ship units, total height is 20 x UNIT_HEIGHT.

fields.name: UNIT_HEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_HEIGHT column.

fields.name: UNIT_HEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_HEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_WEIGHT
fields.description: The weight of the ship unit.

fields.name: UNIT_WEIGHT_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_WEIGHT column.

fields.name: UNIT_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: UNIT_VOLUME
fields.description: it is the volume of each s_ship_unit unit (each row may represent SHIP_UNIT_COUNT number of same ship units). If this field is null, or is 0, the volume of this s_ship_unit is computed as the total of container volume from its ship_unit_spec and the volume of its s_ship_unit_lines.

fields.name: UNIT_VOLUME_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for UNIT_VOLUME column.

fields.name: UNIT_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: UNIT_VOLUME in base units.

fields.name: FLEX_COMMODITY_CODE
fields.description: The user defined commodity code for the item. For example HTS, SITC, STCC, NMFC etc.

fields.name: FLEX_COMMODITY_QUAL_GID
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the tyoe of teh commodity code. For example HTS, SITC, STCC, NMFC etc.

fields.name: IS_SPLITABLE
fields.description: A true or false value to define whether the ship unit can be splitted when creating the shipments.

fields.name: SHIP_UNIT_COUNT
fields.description: Ship units that have the same demension/weight and contents are represented as one row in the SHIP_UNIT table. SHIP_UNIT_COUNT is the number of these ship units.

fields.name: RECEIVED_WEIGHT_BASE
fields.description: RECEIVED_WEIGHT in base units.

fields.name: RECEIVED_VOLUME_BASE
fields.description: RECEIVED_VOLUME in base units.

fields.name: CORE_DIAMETER
fields.description: For cylindrical objects, this defines the diameter of the core

fields.name: CORE_DIAMETER_UOM_CODE
fields.description: For cylindrical objects, unit of measure for the diameter of the core

fields.name: CORE_DIAMETER_BASE
fields.description: For cylindrical objects, the base value of the diameter of the core. For cylindrical objects, the base value of the diameter of the core

fields.name: T_SHIP_UNIT_GID
fields.description: This is used as a non-foreign key reference to the order release ship unit.

fields.name: IS_COUNT_SPLITTABLE
fields.description: Indicates whether the ship unit can be split on the count boundaries when planning during container optimization.

fields.name: MARKED_FOR_PURGE
fields.description: A boolean field. When it is set to "Y", this row and its child data will be purged.

fields.name: IS_ALLOW_REPACK
fields.description: If this flag is set to false, when planning order movement that contains this ship unit or combine shipments that contains this s ship unit, the shipment ship unit building process will not repack this ship unit.

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: This column defines the priority associated with ship unit

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_GID
fields.description: Ship Unit Gid

fields.name: ACCESSORIAL_CODE_GID
fields.description: Accessorial Code gid

(e.g., for 20 ship units that contains 4 cases of 24 cans of coca, each S_SHIP_UNIT_LINE row will have ITEM_GID: coca, ITEM_COUNT 4x24, ITEM_PACKAGE_SPEC_GID: case-of-24-cans, ITEM_PACKAGE_COUNT: 4. In the S_SHIP_UNIT table, the SHIP_UNIT_COUNT will be 20)

tables.description: This table stores the additional remarks at the line item level for the shipment ship unit.

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_GID
fields.description: A unique pointer to define the shipment ship unit. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: REMARK_QUAL_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: PIECE_NUMBER
fields.description: A unique number given to a piece of SSU for the same S_SHIP_UNIT_GID with multiple counts.

fields.name: LOADING_SEQUENCE
fields.description: The loading sequence of the SSU singleton.

fields.name: STACKING_LAYER
fields.description: Stacking layers of the unit. If multiple units are underneath, this is the maximum number of layers underneath added by 1.

fields.name: ORIENTATION_GID
fields.description: Orientation of the ship unit piece.

fields.name: X_COORDINATE
fields.description: The x-coordinate of the unit using container's front lower left corner as the origin.

fields.name: Y_COORDINATE
fields.description: The y-coordinate of the unit using container's front lower left corner as the origin.

fields.name: Z_COORDINATE
fields.description: The z-coordinate of the unit using container's front lower left corner as the origin.

fields.name: TOTAL_TOP_WEIGHT
fields.description: This is the summation of all weights on the top distributed according to the area touching the pieces

fields.name: IS_SPLIT
fields.description: To indicate if this is a loading split from the original SSU.

fields.name: LOAD_CONFIG_3D_PATTERN_GID
fields.description: Corresponding user defined 3d pattern. If the piece is placed using user defined 3d pattern, this indicates the pattern used.

fields.name: PATTERN_INSTANCE_COUNT
fields.description: Instance of the user pattern used

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_GID
fields.description: The ship_unit that is associated with this reference number.

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: The reference number qualifier.

fields.name: REFNUM_VALUE
fields.description: The value of the reference number.

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_REFNUM_QUAL_GID
fields.description: Internal reference qualifier comprised of domain concatenated with the xid.

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_REFNUM_QUAL_XID
fields.description: External reference qualifier.

fields.name: S_SHIP_UNIT_REFNUM_QUAL_DESC
fields.description: Description.

fields.name: IS_VISIBLE_IF_MASTER_CARR
fields.description: Identifies whether the reference numbers with this qualifier should display on related shipments.

fields.name: DEFAULT_REFNUM_BN_TYPE_GID
fields.description: The BN type used to generate a refnum when the refnum field for a record in the corresponding refnum table is not populated.

fields.name: UPDATE_FLAG
fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: This is an internal GC3 table. It groups all the GC3 tables in to logical functioning areas. For e.g., Shipment, Order, Location etc.
This table is associated with Table_Set. It contains the related tables in GC3 that are associated with the functional groups in Table_Set table.

fields.name: OWNER
fields.description: Indicates that the table shown in table_name belongs to OTM or FTI. It wil be considered as OTM if owner is null.

tables.description: This table stores various user defined temperature ranges that can be used to categorize the items and commodities.

fields.name: MIN_TEMP_BASE
fields.description: MIN_TEMP in base units.

fields.name: MAX_TEMP_UOM_CODE
fields.description: Unit of measure for MAX_TEMP column.

fields.name: MAX_TEMP_BASE
fields.description: MAX_TEMP in base units.

tables.description: This tables stores the shipment tender information. Among other things, it contains the service provider name, expected response time and the actual response (Accepted/ Declined) from the service provider.

fields.name: SHIPMENT_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: TENDER_TYPE
fields.description: Defines the type of tender. Values are 'Ordinary', 'Broadcast Tender', and 'Spot Bid'

fields.name: ORIGIN_LOCATION
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: DESTINATION_LOCATION
fields.description: A unique identifier to define the locations withing GC3. The gid contains the domain portion.

fields.name: PLANNED_COST

fields.name: PLANNED_COST_BASE
fields.description: Copied planning cost from shipment in base units.

fields.name: PLANNED_COST_CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: Unit of measure for the planned cost.

tables.description: This table stores the status of the tender as received from the service provider. The status is received as transactions. This table can be linked to the I_Transaction and I_Transmission tables to obtain the details of the status.

fields.name: STATUS_TYPE_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: STATUS_VALUE_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

tables.description: This table contains the information for various service providers to whom the tender collaboration record was sent. In the case of normal tenders there is one record in this table matching to the tender collaboration record. However for broadcast and spot tenders there are multiple (n) records in this table for every tender collaboration record.

fields.name: I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: Foreign key to Tender Collaboration

fields.name: SERVPROV_GID
fields.description: The service provider who received the tender or for spot bidding, the service provider spot bid

fields.name: TRANSACTION_TIME
fields.description: The date of the tender collaboration either tender sent, accept, withdrawal, etc or the last tender spot bid

fields.name: ACCEPTANCE_CODE
fields.description: Either A for Tender Accept or D for Tender Decline

fields.name: RESPONSE_COM_METHOD_GID
fields.description: The communication method used for the collaboration

fields.name: RESPONDING_GL_USER_GID
fields.description: The gluser responding in the collaboration

fields.name: DECLINE_REASON_CODE_GID
fields.description: If decline, the reason for the tender decline

fields.name: DISPATCH_LOCATION_GID
fields.description: The id of the contact of the service provider

fields.name: BID_AMOUNT
fields.description: The amount of the bid indicated by this service provider

fields.name: BID_AMOUNT_CURRENCY_GID
fields.description: The currency of the bid amount

fields.name: BID_AMOUNT_BASE
fields.description: The amount of the bid indicated by this service provider in base units

fields.name: INDICATOR
fields.description: Indicator indicating whether or not this service provider has submitted a valid bid. Values are R (red) and G(Green)

fields.name: IS_WINNER
fields.description: Yes if service provider is the winner, no otherwise. If traditional tender, this is set to Y if / when they accept the tender offer.

fields.name: RESPONSE_I_TRANSACTION_NO
fields.description: store the i_transaction_no of in_bound i_transaction when tender response is sent through integration.

fields.name: PICKUP_TIME
fields.description: Original value is from shipment, if servprov changes pickup time, it is stored in this column.

fields.name: TENDER_COM_METHOD_GID
fields.description: Record the com method used in tender notification.

tables.description: new table for keeping record of tender acceptance by THU entered by servprov. The max acceptance count would be the most recent collaboration.

fields.description: point or span

tables.description: This table is used to indicate which components of the time window should be emphasized. Specify "BOTH" if both early and late delivery dates must be honored. Specify "SHIP" if only ship window need by honored. Specify "DELV" if only delivery window need by honored. Specify "NONE" if GLog should determine the ship and delivery window.

fields.name: TIME_WINDOW_EMPHASIS_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: TIME_WINDOW_EMPHASIS_XID
fields.description: GC3 Unique identifier for the entity. Is used for creating the GID

tables.description: Defines a time point or a time span in a weekly cycle

fields.name: START_TIME
fields.description: Time point start time in seconds, starting from 00:00 midnight.

fields.name: END_TIME
fields.description: End time in seconds, starting from 00:00 midnight.

tables.description: This table is used to define the various time zones across the countries. This is used to schedule shipments and also to achieve full globalization in the GC3. It works with the Time_Zone_Locale table to identify the countries, states and cities that are part of the defined time zones.

fields.name: TIME_ZONE_GID
fields.description: TIME_ZONE_GID contains the unique identifier for a time zone. Examples include Australia/Sydney and Europe/Istanbul.

fields.name: TIME_ZONE_XID
fields.description: TIME_ZONE_XID contains the unique external identifier for the time zone.

fields.name: TIME_ZONE_NAME
fields.description: TIME_ZONE_NAME contains the name of the time zone used for localization.

tables.description: This table is used to define the environment of the time zone, including the countries, states and cities in various time zones across the countries. This is used to schedule shipments and also to achieve full globalization in the GC3.

fields.name: TIME_ZONE_GID
fields.description: TIME_ZONE_GID contains the unique identifier for the time zone for localization.

fields.name: COUNTRY_CODE3_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: CITY
fields.description: CITY contains the name of the city for the time zone.

tables.description: Contains a user-friendly alias for a java class associated with a workflow. Was mainly created so that a workflow could be kicked off by inserting a SQL statement into the PROCESS_CONTROL_REQUEST table through SQL*Plus, rather than having to insert it into GC3 (for testing purposes). Someone kicking it off through SQL*Plus would just need to know the alias, rather than having to type in the whole Java class in the TOPIC_CLASS_NAME column. A workflow will be kicked off whether the TOPIC_CLASS_NAME or the TOPIC_ALIAS_GID is populated within the PROCESS_CONTROL_REQUEST table.

fields.name: TOPIC_ALIAS_GID
fields.description: GC3 Global identifier for the entity. Is created by concatenating Domain Name,"." and XID.

fields.name: TOPIC_ALIAS_XID
fields.description: GC3 Unique identifier for the entity. Is used for creating the GID

fields.name: IS_FOR_PROCESS_CONTROL
fields.description: Specifies if the topic alias is available for use as a process control request.

fields.name: PROCESS_CONTROL_MENU
fields.description: Specifies the hierarchy for the process control menu.

fields.name: IS_PROCESS_SYNC
fields.description: Specifies whether the process is executed or published. Topics which take more than 30 seconds or so should be published to avoid browser timeout. Others should be executed to allow for immediate error reporting.

fields.name: PROCESS_CONTROL_HELP
fields.description: Specifies the online help file for the process topic.

fields.name: IS_FOR_BATCH_PROCESS
fields.description: Specifies if the topic alias is available for use in batch

fields.name: IS_LONG_RUNNING_TASK
fields.description: If Y, this topic is a long running task and should be monitored in the LONG_RUNNING_TASK table.

fields.name: LRT_INTERRUPTABILITY
fields.description: Interruptability support: NONE = task cannot be interrupted; INTERRUPTIBLE = task can be interrupted, likely ending in a failure state; RESUBMITTABLE: task can be resubmitted into it's origin queue for handling by another machine

fields.name: LRT_SCA_REQUIREMENTS
fields.description: For long running tasks, determines if the task should be pulled out of the PROCESS_CONTROL_REQUEST table only when resources are available to process it. NONE=task is pulled regardless of resources; THREAD=task is pulled only if an event thread in the queue is available; MEDIATOR=task is pulled only if an event thread is available and the task will not be blocked by the mediator.

fields.name: LRT_INTERRUPT_ON_SQL
fields.description: If 'Y', a task interruption will force the next SQL statement to fail.

fields.name: THU_PROFILE_GID

tables.description: This table stores the various Unit Of Measures that can be used in the calculations in GC3. Some examples of UOM are: Speed - Mile Per Hour, Kilometer Per Hour etc, Duration - Days, Minutes, Hour etc.

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: TYPE contains the type for the unit of measure. Examples include: DISTANCE DURATION LENGTH SPEED TEMPERATURE VOLUME WEIGHT

fields.name: UOM_CODE
fields.description: UOM_CODE contains an abbreviation for the unit of measure. Examples include CUFT, LB and YD.

fields.name: UOM_SHORT_DESCRIPTION
fields.description: UOM_SHORT_DESCRIPTION contains an abbreviated name for the unit of measure. Examples include CAN GAL, KPH and NAUT MI.

fields.name: UOM_LONG_DESCRIPTION
fields.description: UOM_LONG_DESCRIPTION contains the full name for the unit of measure. Examples include METRIC TONS, POUNDS and YARDS.

fields.name: IS_STORAGE_DEFAULT
fields.description: The IS_STORAGE_DEFAULT column, when set to Y, indicates that the given uom_code is the storage default for all database columns of the given type. For example, LB is the default code for all WEIGHT related columns.

fields.name: IS_DISPLAY_DEFAULT
fields.description: The IS_DISPLAY_DEFAULT column, when set to Y, indicates that the given uom_code is the display default for all display fields of the given type.

tables.description: This table stores the conversion/ map from one Unit of Measure to another Unit Of Measure. For example: For Length the Meter To Feet conversion is 3.2808399, Meter to Yard is 1.0936133 etc.

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: TYPE contains the type for the unit of measure for localization. Examples include: DISTANCE DURATION LENGTH SPEED TEMPERATURE VOLUME WEIGHT

fields.name: FROM_UOM_CODE
fields.description: FROM_UOM_CODE contains an abbreviated name for the original unit of measure being converted.

fields.name: TO_UOM_CODE
fields.description: TO_UOM_CODE contains an abbreviated name for the unit of measure to which another unit of measure is being converted.

fields.name: CONVERSION_CLASS
fields.description: CONVERSION_CLASS contains the Java class name that is used to convert a temperature from one unit of measure to another. The units of measure include Celsius, Fahrenheit and Kelvin.

fields.name: CONVERSION_FACTOR
fields.description: CONVERSION_FACTOR contains the mathematical factors for converting one unit of measure to another.

The service provider"s name is listed in the USER_ASSOCIATION_VALUE. For e.g., MSCARRIERS etc.

Records the reports that a user wants in their workspace.

fields.name: USER_ACCESS_GID
fields.description: Defines the visibility of the workspace records. What this means is that a user_access_gid may be associated with a specific user, a gl_level, or an entire domain. The UI will choose the most granular level of user_access to show to the user. So, if a user wants to create his own workspace, the user_access_gid will have the GL_USER populated, and the user_ws_report_access table will define the list of reports for this workspace. If the user does not create his own workspace, the UI will search at the GL_LEVEL or DOMAIN level to see if a report workspace has been created.

fields.name: REPORT_GID
fields.description: Defines a report within the user's workspace.

fields.name: ICON_GID
fields.description: The icon, or image, associated with the report (foreign keys to ICON table). The image will appear in the user's workspace.

fields.name: DOMAIN_NAME
fields.description: Domain of the User. Is used for creating the GID.

fields.name: INSERT_USER
fields.description: For audit trail purposes. This field stores the id of the user who first entered the record.

fields.name: INSERT_DATE
fields.description: For audit trail purposes. This field stores the date and time when the record was first entered in the database.

fields.name: UPDATE_USER
fields.description: For audit trail purposes. This field stores the id of the user who last updated the record.

fields.name: UPDATE_DATE
fields.description: For audit trail purposes. This field stores the date and time when the record was updated in the database.

fields.description: Specifies if the refnum qualifier can exist on the business object multiple times, or is restricted to occur only once and whether the value is allowed to be updated. If the value is MANY, then the object can have multiple values for the same qualifier. If the value is either UPDATE_OK or UPDATE_NOT_OK, then the rule is in effect, meaning only one value is allowed for a given qualifier. In the case of UPDATE_NOT_OK, the value may not be modified.

tables.description: This table defines the lanes between any two points. The two points can be: - predefined locations (from the Location table), - predefined regions (from the Region and Region Detail tables), - Hierarchical locations (from Geo_hierarchy table), or - a combination of any part of the address that includes City, Province, Postal Code, Country or Zone(s). The zones can be any user defined entries including SPLC (Standard Point Location Codes)

tables.description: Stores detailed information of a slot on a particular row of the yard on a location. Information includes object GID,object type, start time and end time.

fields.name: YARD_GID
fields.description: The unique identifier.

fields.name: LOCATION_GID
fields.description: The Location Identifier.

fields.name: ROW_NUMBER
fields.description: The specific row number of the yard that will be occupied.

fields.name: SLOT_NUMBER
fields.description: The specific slot number of the row on the yard that will be occupied.

fields.name: OBJECT_GID
fields.description: Specify the object ID that will be occupied at a particular slot of a row in the yard. The OBJECT_GID can be null if the slot is blocked.

fields.name: DATA_QUERY_TYPE_GID
fields.description: Specify the object type.

fields.name: START_TIME
fields.description: The start time that the slot is occupied.

fields.name: END_TIME
fields.description: The end time that the slot is occupied.
